programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: All you need in the kitchen for your precision slicing and dicing of fruits and vegetables. Including three cutting inserts, a finger protector, blade holder and a free docking station.

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Dr Sloan sets out to identify the mystery man responsible for threatening an unassuming tax lawyer and jeopardising his position at a prestigious legal firm. Guest starring Leah Remini, with Dick Van Dyke

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Amanda's frightened friend does a disappearing act shortly after giving birth to quadruplets, leaving a flustered Jack Stewart holding the babies - and Dr Sloan investigating the activities of an illegal adoption ring. Dick Van Dyke stars

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: The daughter of the attorney who defended Kimble at his trial tells him she has hired a private detective to help locate the missing one-armed killer.

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.desc: A poison pen note, a man long thought dead and murder add up to courtroom excitement as Perry defends a young heiress accused of killing a blackmailer.

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: When several high-school students are killed, Walker goes undercover as a teacher to investigate

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: Mac and Stella investigate the fatal shooting of a mounted police officer - but the only evidence is a bullet lodged in his horse, presenting them with a dilemma. Aiden takes on the case of a dead youth discovered with severe facial burns, and soon finds herself trying to crack an illegal betting operation

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The death of Special Agent Ned Dorneget hits the team hard, and when Gibbs receives a visit from his late colleague's mother - CIA Officer Joanna Teague - he must decide whether to let her help with the investigation into the Calling. Meanwhile, Gibbs hopes his young charge Luke, formerly one of the Calling's radicalised footsoldiers, might be key to bringing the organisation down. However, when the terrorists discover where their defecting comrade has taken refuge, the abduct him and flee to Iraq. The team follows hot on their heels, but it seems Luke's ties to the Calling might be stronger than anyone at NCIS ever anticipated. Naval crime drama, starring Mark Harmon, with guest Mimi Rogers

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: A naval officer involved in an illegal underground street race is killed when his vehicle explodes. The team's investigations reveal he was carrying classified equipment in relation to his work on a new form of battery that the military had hoped to adopt, and that the explosion - captured on CCTV - is not in accordance with that expected of normal fuel. Meanwhile, Callen latest driving misdemeanour means he must attend traffic school. Chris O'Donnell stars

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: Richard Kimble faces a new ethical problem when he becomes an important witness in a criminal case

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A teenager shoots a convicted drug dealer in front of a group of churchgoers. Stone tries to bring a prosecution - only to find himself faced with a flashy Texas lawyer and an outraged community

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Ducky works on a case involving his estranged childhood best friend, travelling to London with Special Agent Bishop to interview his pal's family and co-workers. While away, the detective reflects on his own past choices, and examines how they have affected his present life. Alice Krige guest stars

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: Walker is haunted by his fiancee's memory when her killer is released from prison. Adventure with the modern-day lawman whose crime-solving methods are rooted firmly in the Old West. Chuck Norris stars

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Homeland Security employee Victor Sterling mysteriously disappears and his car is found at a drive-through restaurant that becomes the scene of a double murder. To complicate matters further, the missing man's outspoken spouse happens to be the ex-wife of both Gibbs and Fornell. American naval crime drama, starring Mark Harmon, Joe Spano, Tom Gallop and Melinda McGraw

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Marine gunnery sergeant Wendell Dobbs is gunned down in a drive-by shooting by assailants wielding AK-47s, and the team suspects the victim may have been involved in illegally importing automatic weapons into the country and supplying them to local gangs. Sam goes undercover to find the truth after learning Dobbs gave up his combat post to mentor his younger brother, who had been hanging out with a gang. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The NCIS team is assigned to a joint terrorism task force by the Secretary when a Moscow-born scientist who it is believed may have been targeted by the Russians for selling state secrets, is found dead. The trail appears to lead to a friend of Sofia's, another scientist who worked for Russia and who may have succumbed to professional jealousy. However, when she too is found dead, the team discovers the hallmarks of Sergei Mishnev, the terrorist who nearly killed Gibbs. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Marine Daniel Zuna is found dead in a pool of his own blood in an alleyway outside a night club, and the team's first stop is to investigate the mixed martial arts gym where the victim spent most of his off-duty time. Kensi poses as Zuna's new girlfriend and suspects the murder could be related to something that happened in Iraq, prompting Sam to go undercover as an MMA fighter in order to see if he can find out the truth. Crime drama, starring LL Cool J and Daniela Ruah

programme.desc: A distant cousin of Fred's who has Down's syndrome is taken in by the Buckles following the death of his protective mother, but Violet struggles to cope and Shelagh is forced to make other arrangements for his care. New recruit Valerie prepares for her first day at Nonnatus House, and Trixie comes to the aid of a pregnant woman who is in urgent need of dental treatment to avoid an infection. The nurses discover what has happened to Sister Mary Cynthia, a revelation that has distressing consequences for Sister Monica Joan

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: While the world anxiously awaits the resolution of the Cuban Missile Crisis, Valerie treats a pregnant Somali woman who has undergone female genital circumcision. The Nonnatus Team arrange for her to go into hospital, but she responds by going into hiding, and the situation is complicated further when it emerges that the woman's younger sister will soon go through the same process. Sister Mary Cynthia's depression is treated with electroconvulsive therapy, which she hopes will restore her faith

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Gerry celebrates his birthday at a comedy club, where a controversial act comes under fire from a group of hecklers. Sandra discovers the most vocal member of the gang is the niece of a political activist who went missing 25 years previously after waging a campaign against the comedian. Agreeing to reinvestigate the disappearance, the squad discovers that the activist and her boyfriend had infiltrated an extreme right-wing group with links to the dangerous criminal who murdered Jack's wife. Guest starring Phil Daniels, Joe Absolom and John Henshaw

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: The murder of Amir Aziz, a prominent anti-fundamentalist Muslim, drags the Lyell team into the murky world of hate-crime and counter-terrorism. Initially thought to be the work of a EDL member whose son was killed by the Taliban, the team begin to suspect the killing is connected to Sarah Begovic, a young woman who left the UK to relocate to Syria, but who recently returned home in secret with her six-month-old son. programme.desc: New surgical consultant Linden Cullen arrives to lead the team on AAU, leaving Lola unsure of her position. Sam struggles on Darwin as he tries to work through his chemotherapy, and Ric is frustrated first by Michael's ability to get his private patients priority treatment and then at his secret plans to open a private ward in the hospital

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Dillon is shocked as Lara receives her sentence and Simon lands himself in hot water when his love life starts to interfere with his work. Nikki is saluted for her recent bravery and seizes the opportunity to publicise the dangers facing paramedics, while a distracted Josh allows his marriage to suffer. Drama with the staff of Holby A&E, starring Christine Stephen-Daly, Dan Rymer and Ian Bleasdale

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Denise Welch guest stars as a mother distressed to learn her 13-year-old daughter has overdosed on heroin. Phil Hunter arrests some of the dealer's gang in the local park, but there is further heartbreak in store for the beleaguered family. Roger Valentine's life starts to fall apart as his estranged wife files for divorce, and a cloud hangs over the celebrations as Sun Hill bids farewell to new Borough Commander Adam Okaro

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Grant wakes up to find that Sharon has signed the divorce papers and gone to stay with Angie in Florida, leaving Michelle to run the Vic

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Sanjay is betting and Debbie wonders if Gita knows. Bianca is bored by Ricky. Pat refuses to go out with Roy Evans. Bianca starts work in the chippy and the pub runs out of beer

programme.title: London's Burning
programme.desc: The team races against time to put out a fire in a hospital which was started by a porter in an arson attack. Starring Glen Murphy and Ben Onwukwe

programme.title: Peak Practice
programme.desc: Clare begins to lose her grip on reality altogether, while David has an unscheduled run-in with glamorous old flame Anna Bradshaw, and it isn't long before sparks are flying again - in more ways than one. Adrian Lukis, Caroline Langrishe and Gary Mavers star

programme.title: Dangerfield
programme.desc: A girl accused of noise pollution is found strangled - but the obvious suspect, a man who complained about her in the first place and was seen standing over the body, has understandably made himself scarce. Back at the surgery, Shaaban decides he's had enough and walks out. Drama, starring Nigel Havers and Adam Cockerton

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Sharon and Tracey's school reunion brings back a few memories and a chance to bury the hatchet, while Dorien does some reminiscing of her own - with a toy-boy lover from her past. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph

programme.title: Waiting for God
programme.desc: A marriage proposal leads to Diana being loaded into an ambulance, and Harvey becomes set on the idea of becoming a priest. Comedy, starring Stephanie Cole

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!
programme.desc: Rene gets rid of the money and tries to dispose of the microfilm, only to be hampered by naughty Resistance girls

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Compo unwittingly bags a jumble sale bargain, an item that transforms him from an old man into a heart-breaking macho man - with a little help from his friends. Comedy, starring Bill Owen and Frank Thornton

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Boxing Day sees heavy snowfall covering the country and the adverse weather causes major disruption across Poplar as roads become blocked and pipes freeze meaning families have to queue outside for water from standpipes. Sister Julienne must inform a family about the death of their loved one and uncovers an uncomfortable truth, while Valerie helps an unmarried couple living in a caravan prepare for their baby, but unforeseen complications knock her confidence in her ability as a midwife. Guest starring Anita Dobson

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Gerry is forced to confront his past when the team reinvestigates the 33-year-old murder of a doctor whose body was found hanging from a meat hook on a butcher's stall at Smithfield Market. The butcher had gone missing on the same day, and since then the community has rallied to help his widow keep the business afloat. However, his recently discovered remains reveal a match to an unidentified person and lead to a shocking revelation for Sandra. Guest starring Ken Farrington, David Calder and Diana Quick, with Amanda Redman and Alun Armstrong

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: The accidental fatal shooting of a Polish teenager in an armed police operation sparks a series of murders, which links Turkish gangsters, a grieving family and a fractured firearms unit. As the inquest delivers a lawful killings verdict, IPCC investigator Vicky Sharpe calls on the Lyell team to investigate the connection between the events and review the police evidence, but Sharpe's relationship with the family of the teenager and her suspicion of the firearms division lead Nikki and Thomas to distrust her motives. Feature-length episode, starring Emilia Fox

programme.title: The Evacuees
programme.desc: Jack Rosenthal's 1975 drama, directed by Alan Parker, chronicling the experiences of two Jewish boys evacuated from London to avoid the horrors of the Blitz. Maureen Lipman, Margery Mason and Ray Mort star

While there, the presenter heads to the seaside town of Worthing, and finds out how one of the oldest working cinemas in the country has been saved from redevelopment

programme.title: Call That Hard Work?

programme.title: Our Lives
programme.desc: Following the life of 57-year-old Lorraine, a tribute act to the American pop star, Pink, exploring what it takes to spend your life pretending to be someone else

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The team restores a rusty toy pedal car that is missing its steering wheel, a Victorian stamp perforating machine, a bronze statue of a dagger-wielding assassin by leading French artist Claire Colinet, and a baize-covered card table dating from the 1940s that has sentimental value for its owner

programme.title: Reported Missing
programme.desc: When his father misses an appointment with his mental health worker, 25-year-old Oli calls Gloucestershire Police, concerned about the whereabouts of his father, Michael, who suffers from paranoid schizophrenia and is thought to have stopped taking his medication. With no sign of Michael overnight, police launch an appeal for information, and CCTV searches of the town reveal that Michael got into a taxi two nights before and was taken to a high point in the Cotswold's. Officers prepare for a major search operation, but looking for someone hiding in a heavily wooded area presents them with major challenges

programme.title: Fort Salem
programme.desc: Supernatural drama set in an alternate America, following three young witches from basic training in combat magic into early deployment on the military frontline to fight against a dangerous rebel group called The Spree. Starring Taylor Hickson, Jessica Sutton and Ashley Nicole Williams

programme.title: Fort Salem
programme.desc: The unit travels to Salem Town for an annual witch celebration, but an unexpected disturbance throws the proceedings into chaos. Meanwhile, Tally wonders if she made the right choice to embark on a military career. Supernatural drama, starring Taylor Hickson and Jessica Sutton

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: John Torode and Gregg Wallace look back on further highlights from 15 years of the show, remembering some of the most entertaining team challenges - and how different dynamics can lead to either success or chaos in the kitchen. They also revisit a 2015 semi-final task which saw Sheree Murphy, Scott Maslen, Rylan Clark-Neal, Sam Nixon, Chesney Hawkes, Mica Paris, Kimberly Wyatt and Tom Parker thrown together in a Victorian-style village, using only ingredients widely available during that era

programme.title: Breakfast

Later, there's a tense atmosphere at Thomas Watsons auctioneers. Plus, host Anita Manning catches up with Charles Hanson and a very special musical collection.

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: Kam acts out of character at the school play, and Max quits chemotherapy

programme.title: Escape to the Country

programme.title: Fake Britain
programme.desc: Dom Littlewood and Michelle Ackerley investigate the fake Botox treatment that left one woman with life changing injuries. Elsewhere, a report on false online reviews that are ruining business across the UK, deadly fake camping stoves that can explode when they are lit, and Michelle tests imitation Airpods headphones

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: John Torode and Gregg Wallace recall some of the best - and the worst - reviews of the celebrities' creations. From members of the Women's Institute to previous finalists and the forthright opinions of the restaurant critics, the role of the external opinion is a crucial one. John and Gregg also revisit a classic challenge from 2011, in which the five semi-finalists were tasked with cooking the much-loved recipes from residents of a retirement village

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: The president of the local cricket club is found shot dead in the middle of the pitch after a recent victory. Suspicion falls on his family and fellow players, but all of them have an alibi. JP thinks Dwayne is keeping something from him and wonders how well he knows his friend, and Humphrey dreads Martha's imminent departure from the island

programme.title: Fighting the Power: Britain After George Floyd
programme.desc: Daniel Henry follows the Black Lives Matter campaign in the UK, as the country is forced to look again at its record on race in the wake of the death of George Floyd in the US

programme.title: Reported Missing
programme.desc: When his father misses an appointment with his mental health worker, 25-year-old Oli calls Gloucestershire Police, concerned about the whereabouts of his father, Michael, who suffers from paranoid schizophrenia and is thought to have stopped taking his medication. With no sign of Michael overnight, police launch an appeal for information, and CCTV searches of the town reveal that Michael got into a taxi two nights before and was taken to a high point in the Cotswold's. Officers prepare for a major search operation, but looking for someone hiding in a heavily wooded area presents them with major challenges

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Presented by Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Ben Fogle observes a Cuban crocodile death roll for the first time, while the keepers face a challenge from a pride of lairy lions and Jean Johansson meets some chatty parrots

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Properties in West Bromwich, Leeds and Croydon, all bought without viewing ahead of the auction, are sold under the hammer, and the owners reveal whether they regret their buys

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: A 95-year-old navy veteran is winched from the deck of a cruise ship, having lain undiscovered in his cabin for 11 hours. Plus, the dental team has to take to the road to get to patients across Shetland

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles is at the helm with experts Danny Sebastian and Caroline Hawley, as two teams scour the antiques centre in the hope that they can make the greater profit at auction. There are plenty of highs and lows in the saleroom, while Eric also gets the chance to meet an unlikely war hero

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: Kam is schooled in privilege, and Leonie makes excuses to stick with Erika. Fiona Press and Mitchell Bourke star

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: Consumer journalists Jasmine Birtles, Chris Hollins, Simon Calder, Adam Pearson and Rebecca Wilcox compete in a charity edition hosted by Jeremy Vine

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton is tasked with helping a couple and their young sons quit London to make a new life for themselves in Derbyshire, with a budget of £475,000. He also spends time with an artisan craftsman who is bringing back the local skill of knife-making

programme.title: Call That Hard Work?
programme.desc: Poultry farmer Andrew, restaurant manager Angela and performer Jack go head to head in a bid to prove their job is the hardest. The trio get off to a cracking start sorting thousands of eggs on poultry farmer Andrew's smallholding in Berkshire. While performer Jack takes his new role a little too slowly, restaurant manager Angela finds herself in a total scramble. The next day, both Andrew and Jack struggle to memorise four menus as they prepare to serve 40 covers at the high-end Indian restaurant where Angela works. For their final day, both the poultry farmer and restaurant manager face their fears to perform in front of a 45-strong audience at top tourist attraction the London Dungeon. Narrated by Sunetra Sarker

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Philip Serrell and Stephanie Connell embark on the last leg of their journey. Steph tries her hand at playing the bagpipes in Hexham, Northumberland, while Phil finds out how coal mining led to the development of Britain's rail network. The duo then meet at the final auction in Darlington, Co Durham

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Ronan Keating acts as co-host alongside Alex Jones for the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: Wallace & Gromit: A Matter of Loaf and Death
programme.desc: The duo open their own bakery, Top Bun, and business is soon booming - not least because a 'cereal killer' is targeting all the rival bakers in town. Even better, Wallace has caught the eye of local beauty Piella Bakewell. But Gromit is more concerned that his master might become the killer's next victim. With the voices of Peter Sallis and Sally Lindsay

programme.title: The Royal Edinburgh Military Tattoo
programme.desc: Bill Paterson narrates highlights of the event held on the esplanade of Edinburgh Castle, with the RAF College Band taking centre stage as the year's home service. Others featured include the Combined Bands of the Royal Cavalry of the Sultanate of Oman, the United States Air Force Honor Guard Drill Team, the Royal Marines Band Plymouth, the Band of the Royal Regiment of Scotland, the Central Band of the Czech Armed Forces and the Banda Monumental de Mexico, the Tattoo Dance Company and the Top Secret Drum Corps

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Guest panellists Jack Dee, Romesh Ranganathan, Gaby Roslin and Tinchy Stryder join team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy show in which participants attempt to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves. Hosted by Rob Brydon

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Conor and Jock decide it's time to move out of home and head to Spain to run a bar. First to receive the Spain news is Mairead, who is in disbelief - how is she going to keep digging them out of holes when they're living overseas? Billy Murphy receives unexpected information that causes him to faint - resulting in a large lump on his head and concern for Mairead and the lads. Then, Sergeant Healy arrives at the door with a barbershop quartet serenading Mairead as he's about to propose. Chris Walley, Alex Murphy and Hilary Rose star

programme.title: The Witches of Eastwick (1987)
programme.desc: Three single women dabble in magic to conjure up the perfect lover, and unintentionally summon Satan himself, who seduces them one by one. They come to realise the danger the lecherous devil presents and decide to end their relationships, but the infernal rogue does not take kindly to rejection. Fantasy comedy based on John Updike's novel, with Jack Nicholson, Cher, Michelle Pfeiffer and Susan Sarandon

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Call That Hard Work?
programme.desc: A plumber, a tour guide and a dog groomer compete to prove their profession is the toughest, with each having to spend a day working at the other's jobs

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Kate Humble meets an ostrich that thinks it is a zebra, Ben Fogle takes a test to identify all the park's guinea pigs by name, and Jean Johansson watches lions climbing trees

programme.title: Amazing Hotels: Life Beyond the Lobby
programme.desc: Monica Galetti and Giles Coren visit Schloss Elmau, a hotel located deep in the snowy Bavarian Alps that is promoted as providing a cultural hideaway that allows guests the chance to restore their mind, body and soul. Monica joins in one of the meditation sessions held every day for the restaurant staff, and Giles recites poetry and travels to a mountain hut to serve dinner, while dressed in lederhosen. They join the hotel guests for an evening performance featuring world-renowned musicians playing for free

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: The Fearons, from Dagenham in east London, sample life in Australia. Husband Sheldon feels such a permanent move will help his family enjoy a better pace of life, but wife Shelly Ann is struggling with leaving her life in the UK behind. Presented by Nicki Chapman

programme.title: The Boss
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Susan Calman in which one contestant has to use judgement and tactics to decide which other player is best suited to answer questions

programme.title: Curious Creatures
programme.desc: Kate Humble hosts the panel show that tests the wildlife knowledge of team captains Chris Packham and Lucy Cooke, with guests Vim Kumaratunga and Lindsey Chapman

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: George is still depressed about James's rejection of life at Ash Park, while Sarah realises he is neglecting David. Elizabeth ignores an important note from Douglas's solicitor

programme.title: A to Z of TV Gardening
programme.desc: Carol Kirkwood looks at subjects beginning with the letter I, continuing her alphabetical journey through the world of horticulture. Along the way, she gets inspiration and advice from some of the BBC's most popular gardening presenters

programme.title: Life in the Air
programme.desc: The series turns to animals that fly, including footage of half a million mother bats, an agile sparrowhawk raiding for food, and large beetles taking to the sky as part of their mating ritual. The programme also reveals how peregrine falcons are able to reach speeds of 200mph, and why an albatross's nose helps it to fly. Narrated by Suranne Jones

programme.title: World's Weirdest Events
programme.desc: Chris Packham discovers the secrets behind a flame-filled firenado, unearths a strange-looking living rock and meets a team of oversized rats who save lives by sniffing out landmines

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: In Blackburn, Anita Manning values a gold charm bracelet and Philip Serrell takes a shine to an unusual globe. Paul Martin finds out about the life of singer Kathleen Ferrier

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Angellica Bell, Gyles Brandreth, Scarlett Moffatt and Iain Stirling test their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games this week

programme.title: Gardening Together with Diarmuid Gavin
programme.desc: The horticultural designer fashions a garden for the Hanway family in Howth, who have a shed-load of ideas for their patch, but no firm plan. For those wanting to live the good life themselves, Diarmuid gets advice from chef Rachel Allen, who helps him fulfil his dream of keeping chickens in the garden

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Strictly Come Dancing 2016 champions Ore Oduba and Joanne Clifton swap the ballroom for the saleroom as they travel around Sussex and Kent in search of bargains, with the help of experts Catherine Southon and David Harper. Along the way, Ore learns how cricket has its origins in a game invented by milk maids 600 years ago, and Joanne finds out about vintage movie-making in Shoreham-by-Sea. But which of the duo will make the most profit when they go to auction in Southend?

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: On a railway journey from the capital Palermo, through the ancient town of Agrigento and the port of Siracusa, to Europe's largest volcano, Mount Etna, Michael explores Sicilian life under the dictatorship. He also finds out how the dictator took on the Mafia and asks whether it is true that under Mussolini, the trains ran on time

programme.title: Harlots
programme.desc: Period drama set in London in 1763 following a woman as she struggles to reconcile her roles as mother and brothel owner. The body of Sir George Howard is found and given the testimony of Thomas Haxby, the main suspects are Charlotte Wells and Daniel Marney, who were seen at his property and - according to him - were covered in mud and blood. In attempting to save one daughter, Margaret has seemingly condemned the other. Starring Hugh Skinner, Edward Hogg, Jessica Brown Findlay and Rory Fleck Bryne

programme.title: Harlots
programme.desc: Daniel and Charlotte await trial for the murder of Sir George. Lydia tries to take down Margaret Wells once and for all, while Harriet unites with North in one final attempt to steal her children back from Benjamin Lennox. Starring Rory Fleck Byrne, Jessica Brown Findlay and Hugh Skinner

programme.title: Grand Tours of Scotland
programme.desc: Paul Murton goes on a caving expedition under the summit of Blaven

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Katie Razzall

programme.title: Manctopia: Billion Pound Property Boom
programme.desc: Local property developer Tim hits a million-pound problem in the mill he's converting into luxury apartments, while less than a mile away Anne and her friends face losing their homes to a giant redevelopment scheme. A homelessness support charity Lifeshare is forced to leave the Victorian Charter Street Mission, which it has operated out of for more than 30 years, due to redevelopment of the surrounding area. However, new businesses are also relocating to the city, which leads to two Glasgow IT workers looking for accommodation

programme.title: Inside the Bruderhof
programme.desc: Documentary telling the story of a community of radical Christians living a remarkable back-to-basics life in a quiet British village. The 300 residents of Darvell in Sussex - one of 23 Bruderhof communities around the world - live outside mainstream society. They have relinquished money and possessions to live as disciples as Jesus, running an almost self-sufficient community. The film follows three of the residents, including inquisitive 18-year-old Hannah, who is starting to question her way of life. Before making a lifelong commitment, she journeys to London to find out how others live

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: Three talented artists to make something special for the homes of people they've never met. In Lincolnshire, a buyer is looking for a gift for her husband as a thank-you for all he does for the family and to mark his long service to charity work. Will a stone sculptor, a mosaic maker or an illustrator create the perfect present? Presented by Nick Knowles

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein re-examine the 1934 case of an off-duty soldier in financial difficulties who was convicted of murdering a friend on New Year's Eve

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Call That Hard Work?
programme.desc: Tree surgeon Olly, sandwich factory supervisor Ruth and zookeeper Charlotte compete, taking turns at doing one another's jobs before one of them walks away with the £1,000 prize

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: The park sees the formation of its first ever all female wolf pack, and Ben gets a peek at some incredible footage of male koala, Burke

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels from the chateaux of the Loire Valley to the heart of the Champagne region at Reims, beginning in the city of Orleans in north-central France. Here, he follows his Bradshaw's guide to the magnificent stained-glass windows of the Cathedral of Sainte-Croix, which tell the story of the heroine of France, Joan of Arc. Later, Michael takes a spin around the track at Le Mans in a French-built car, before making his way to the capital city of Paris. East of Paris, Michael finishes this leg of his journey in style with a tour of the cellars at Domaine Pommery in Reims

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Gavin and Aimee Stockley and their two children sample life in Melbourne for a week, hoping that a move to Australia will enable them to put a difficult few years behind them

programme.title: The Boss
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Susan Calman in which one contestant becomes the Boss and has to use judgement and tactics to decide which player is best suited to answer questions

programme.title: Curious Creatures
programme.desc: Team captains Chris Packham and Lucy Cooke are joined by Steve Leonard and Miranda Krestovnikoff for another test of wildlife knowledge, hosted by Kate Humble

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: Douglas' son Matthew explains to Elizabeth why his father refused to acknowledge him as he makes the acquaintance of both Anna and Olivia. George is convinced that Regina's plan to distract Sir Richard whilst he finds another source to cover his debts will succeed, but the family will be reliant on Jack's cooperation. Romantic drama, starring Marta Dusseldorp

programme.title: A to Z of TV Gardening
programme.desc: Carol Kirkwood looks at subjects beginning with the letter R, continuing her alphabetical journey through the world of horticulture. Along the way, she gets inspiration and advice from some of the BBC's most popular gardening presenters

programme.title: Life in the Air
programme.desc: The final episode of the series features footage shot from within a giant flock of birds, revealing how half a million of them can fly in a group without colliding with each other. The programme also showcases the world's fastest airborne courtship display, a bird that savagely defends its territory, and conflict between moths and bats in the South American jungle

programme.title: World's Weirdest Events
programme.desc: Chris Packham presents more amazing facts and fun, discovering the secrets behind mysterious holes in the Russian tundra, finding an underwater lake and meeting the creator of a cannon built especially for salmon

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: At Blackburn Cathedral, Charles Hanson values a rare Eureka clock and Anita Manning is drawn to four oil paintings of the Ribble Valley. Host Paul Martin meets up with Michelin-star chef Nigel Haworth to discover how to make the perfect Lancashire hotpot

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Angellica Bell, Gyles Brandreth, Scarlett Moffatt and Iain Stirling test their knowledge in trivia-based games across the week, with the ultimate winner declared on Friday

programme.title: Gardening Together with Diarmuid Gavin
programme.desc: Diarmuid helps design a garden for Nichola McGregor in Co Antrim, who wants to bring a bit of France to her garden in memory of her late partner. He is joined by exotic plant expert Conrad McCormick, and shows how to rejuvenate a pond

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Accompanied by experts James Braxton and Raj Bisram, former Holby City actors Tina Hobley and Sharon Maughan traverse the Cotswold countryside in search of some classy antiques to sell at auction. Sharon breaks from the shops to learn about a troubled love life of one the country's greatest artists, while Tina finds out why the wealthy once raced their cars up a hill at break-neck speed

programme.title: Glow Up: Britain's Next Make-Up Star
programme.desc: It's the semi-final and just four MUAs remain to battle it out for a place in the grand final. This week's assignment requires serious flair as the MUAs are booked on a beauty editorial for renowned photographer and director - and this week's guest judge - Rankin. His magazine, The Hunger, is known for pushing the boundaries of fashion, style and make-up and he wants the MUAs to think experimental beauty, express themselves and most importantly inspire him for today's beauty editorial with the theme of 'The Future'. Presented by Stacey Dooley

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: A further selection of highlights from the stand-up show focusing on a different topic each week. This time around, Rob Brydon, Sara Pascoe, Dara O Briain, Lou Sanders, Rich Hall, Dane Baptiste and others offer their thoughts on sex

programme.title: Mandy
programme.desc: It is dinner at Trent Manor, and everyone around the table has a motive for murdering Lord Trent. But who did? Mandy wasn't invited to the manor, so she is off doing something else. Comedy short, written, directed by and starring Diane Morgan, with guest star David Bradley

programme.title: Mandy
programme.desc: Mandy rings radio DJ Iain Lee and tells him how she became trapped in a coffin, having been forced into a robbery. Comedy short written, directed by and starring Diane Morgan, with Natalie Cassidy

programme.title: Semi-Detached
programme.desc: Lee Mack's put-upon anti-hero struggles his way through yet another farcical 30 minutes of his life in the fourth episode of the real-time sitcom. This time around, The dodgy-dealing antics of Stuart's brother Charlie finally catch up with the pair of them. April's ex-fiance shows up wanting her back, and Barry plans a huge surprise for Sandy. Co-starring former Phoenix Nights star and writer Neil Fitzmaurice as Charlie, alongside Christian Brassington, Cavan Clerkin, Geoff McGivern and Ellie White

programme.title: Squeamish About…
programme.desc: Michael Squeamish offers his unique take on the countryside, shining a distorted light on ancient customs and modern rural life. From Stonehenge to the rights and rituals of the Druids, May Day festivals to the still-popular pastime of gurning, he offers some distinctive opinions on Britain's farmlands and the folk who live there. Squeamish uses a unique selection of archive footage, and plays fast-and-loose with the facts, to create a joyously surreal whistle-stop tour of Britain's relationship with the countryside

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Mark Urban

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul Whitehouse and Bob Mortimer return to Scotland for their latest fishing trip. Having failed to catch a salmon in the River Tay, they hope to have more luck on the Tweed. They begin their journey at the isolated Bemersyde Estate before moving on to the Bridge Pool at Kelso, one of the most famous salmon fishing spots in Scotland. They also try their hand at boat fishing and receive some mystical predictions from a familiar face

programme.title: Harry Hill's World of TV
programme.desc: The comedian offers his unique take on different TV genres, beginning with a guide to the history of soap operas. Harry touches on everything from The Grove Family and The Newcomers to EastEnders and Coronation Street, while also giving viewers a few helpful tips on how to make their own domestic drama

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring four more treasured family heirlooms, and the memories they hold, back to life, tackling a radio that survived Dunkirk, a battered leather gaming case, a grandfather clock with a poignant history, and a pair of Viking statues

programme.title: Stories of Us
programme.desc: The Rev Kate Bottley and Ashley John-Baptiste explore the benefits of nature, with a trip to the woods and a day spent working on a farm

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein reinvestigate the notorious shooting of policeman PC George William Gutteridge in Essex in 1927

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Call That Hard Work?
programme.desc: Stable manager Gemma, tea-room owner Frieda and commercial fisherman Pete compete, taking turns at doing one another's jobs before one of them walks away with the £1,000 prize

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Ben Fogle and Kate Humble find out how moving will encourage the secretary birds to mate, and serve a meal to a wolf. Jean Johansson learns how to determine a scorpion's gender

programme.title: Bears About the House
programme.desc: Part two of two. Conservationist Giles Clark continues his biggest mission to date - taking on the illegal wildlife trade and helping to build a new bear sanctuary in Laos, south-east Asia. At the sanctuary, Mary the sun bear is now nine months old. Now growing in confidence, she is becoming extremely determined, and picks up a skill unique to a sun bear, jumping. Later, the sanctuary receives some shocking news when they discover there has been a break-in, and the distraught team have to come to terms with a theft

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Seeing her daughter cope with cerebral palsy has inspired Bradford mum Nicola that a new life in Perth, Australia is the way forward. Nicki Chapman follows the family's progress

programme.title: The Boss
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Susan Calman in which one contestant becomes the Boss and has to use judgement and tactics to decide which player is best suited to answer questions

programme.title: Curious Creatures
programme.desc: Kate Humble hosts as team captains Chris Packham and Lucy Cooke are joined by Vianet Djenguet and Steve Backshall for another test of wildlife knowledge

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: Regina warns Jack that Sir Richard is intending to use his friendship with Frank Gibbs to incite bigots in the election, while George reveals his intent to marry Sarah

programme.title: A to Z of TV Gardening
programme.desc: Carol Kirkwood looks at subjects beginning with the letter T, continuing her alphabetical journey through the world of horticulture. Along the way, she gets inspiration and advice from some of the BBC's most popular gardening presenters

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Asian Adventure
programme.desc: Dave Myers and Si King continue their culinary journey around Asia by heading to Thailand, where they discover why Bangkok has a reputation as the street food capital of the world, and help serve up school dinners. Heading out into the country's central plains, the duo join paddy-field workers and find out how rice is grown

programme.title: Animal Super Parents
programme.desc: A look at the fascinating behaviours exhibited by some of the natural world's most dedicated parents, beginning with a look at those animals that are tasked with raising their offspring alone. Examples include the slow loris covering her baby in toxic saliva, the Weddell seal putting her pup through winter boot camp, a weedy sea dragon camouflaging his eggs against his body and the giant Pacific octopus sacrificing herself for her brood. Narrated by Hugh Dennis

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: At Dover Castle in Kent, Jonathan Pratt and James Lewis scour the crowd to find objects to take off to auction. Jonathan discovers an elegant Lalique figure and James finds a pristine collection of Chinese snuff bottles. Presenter Paul Martin uncovers the murderous history of the castle

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Angellica Bell, Gyles Brandreth, Scarlett Moffatt and Iain Stirling go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games, vying to be crowned weekly champion

programme.title: Gardening Together with Diarmuid Gavin
programme.desc: Diarmuid helps design a garden in Co Kerry for Shane Auliffe, who wants to see a formal, grand garden from his window. He offers advice for gardeners with a bit of help from Mount Stewart head gardener Neil Porteous and shows how to transform a terrace area, as he makes the finishing touches to his own garden

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: TV presenters Anthea Turner and Andi Peters join experts Charles Hanson and Margie Cooper on a quest to find items to sell at auction. Their search takes them through Cheshire, Greater Manchester and Staffordshire, with Margie allowing Andi to indulge his passion for aviation when he swaps their classic car for a vintage Vulcan bomber, while Anthea learns about Staffordshire's rich heritage and why the county is synonymous with pottery

programme.title: BBC Proms 2020
programme.desc: Katie Derham presents the First Night of the live classical music festival, with a feast of performances including Beethoven's Third Symphony, Aaron Copland's Quiet City, and a new work by young British composer Hannah Kendall, all performed by the BBC Symphony Orchestra and BBC Singers, under conductor Sakari Oramo. Katie and special guest Stephen Fry also celebrate the return of live music to the Royal Albert Hall

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: From giant gooseberries and growing the perfect sweet pea to flamboyant dahlias and plants pollinated by a single human hair, Monty Don celebrates the plant passions of some of the extraordinary people that the programme has met over the last few years. He also has plenty of hints and tips of his own to keep enthusiastic gardeners busy over the weekend ahead

programme.title: State of the Union
programme.desc: Tom and Louise look back on their early love life, and try to decide if they can see a future together. Comedy written by Nick Hornby and directed by Stephen Frears about a couple who fear their marriage is failing and decide to give counselling sessions a try. Starring Chris O'Dowd and Rosamund Pike

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Mark Urban

programme.title: Cricket: Today at the Test
programme.desc: Action from the first T20 match between England and Pakistan at Old Trafford

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Nazanin Zaghari-Ratcliffe has been held prisoner in Iran for more than four years after being convicted of spying charges. Nobody knows how many other British citizens have been imprisoned by the Iranian authorities, because the UK government refuses to say. Reporter Darragh MacIntyre meets the families of some of those who have been arbitrarily detained and asks whether the payment of a historic debt could set them free.

programme.title: Cuba: Castro vs the World
programme.desc: Part two of two. A look at the rocky relationship between Castro and Bill Clinton in the wake of the end of the Cold War, as the loss of Soviet support proved devastating for Cuba's economy and its people, leading to mass immigration to Florida. The programme also examines how Castro assisted Hugo Chavez' rise to power in Venezuela in exchange for oil, and the transition of power from an ageing Castro to his younger brother. Featuring contributions from key figures in this political era, including Clinton himself

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Johnny Kingdom - A Year on Exmoor
programme.desc: A quiet January for the amateur cameraman soon turns busy when wild boar become loose in the Devonshire countryside, bringing Britain's media to the area. Johnny also films spoonbills, finding a unique way to get close to the migrating birds

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross demonstrates how to paint a night scene, with waves crashing against rocks and a lighthouse

programme.title: Monty Don's Japanese Gardens
programme.desc: Part two of two. The horticultural enthusiast explores Japan during autumn. He begins as he did in the spring, at one of the 'three great gardens of Japan' to learn how they are protected from the coming winter. Monty follows the history of the Japanese garden through the military strongholds of past leaders to the many styles, new and old, of the iconic stroll garden and finds a slice of Victorian England in the heart of Tokyo. He also learns how the Japanese are weaving nature into their concrete urban jungle. He ends his journey by the Sea of Japan at a place that makes art out of its gardens

programme.title: Japan: Earth's Enchanted Islands
programme.desc: In the south-west of Japan, the Ryukyu Islands stretch towards the tropics and all life on them is influenced by the power of the sea, with volcanos and typhoons forces to be reckoned with. Michelle Dockery narrates a journey beginning at an island at the top of the chain and travelling south, revealing unexpected stories of isolation, unique wildlife and unsolved mysteries

programme.title: The Art of Japanese Life
programme.desc: James Fox explores how the art and culture produced by cities past and present has shaped the country's attitudes and helped forge the very idea of Japan itself

programme.title: The Art of Japanese Life
programme.desc: James Fox explores the art of the Japanese home, the clean minimalism of which has been exported round the world from modernist architecture to lifestyle stores

programme.title: People's Palaces: The Golden Age of Civic Architecture
programme.desc: Part one of two. Architectural historian Dr Jonathan Foyle explores some of the most impressive Georgian and Victorian buildings in the north of England, beginning with neo-classical constructions. He visits town and concert halls, libraries, schools and galleries in Liverpool, Leeds, Bradford and Manchester to find out how the regular proportion and symmetry of the style was intended to suggest order in chaotically expanding urban environments

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross demonstrates how to paint a night scene, with waves crashing against rocks and a lighthouse

programme.title: Johnny Kingdom - A Year on Exmoor
programme.desc: A quiet January for the amateur cameraman soon turns busy when wild boar become loose in the Devonshire countryside, bringing Britain's media to the area. Johnny also films spoonbills, finding a unique way to get close to the migrating birds

programme.title: The Beauty of Maps
programme.desc: Cartography through the ages, beginning with the Hereford Mappa Mundi, the largest intact medieval wall map in the world, which was intended to picture all of human knowledge in a single image. The programme traces its history, and features interviews with people who have been affected by it, including Turner Prize-winning artist Grayson Perry

programme.title: Japan: Earth's Enchanted Islands
programme.desc: In the south-west of Japan, the Ryukyu Islands stretch towards the tropics and all life on them is influenced by the power of the sea, with volcanos and typhoons forces to be reckoned with. Michelle Dockery narrates a journey beginning at an island at the top of the chain and travelling south, revealing unexpected stories of isolation, unique wildlife and unsolved mysteries

programme.title: Johnny Kingdom - A Year on Exmoor
programme.desc: Spring proves a busy time for the amateur cameraman, but his mind is elsewhere as his pet deer Bambi falls sick. Johnny rescued the three-legged animal 12 years ago, but her illness leaves him facing a difficult decision

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross creates a painting of a large old mill in the heart of a forest

programme.title: Girl Friday
programme.desc: Another chance to see this documentary following Absolutely Fabulous star Joanna Lumley as she attempts to survive on a desert island off Madagascar with only a few basic tools to help her

programme.title: Brideshead Revisited (2008)
programme.desc: A student at Oxford in the 1920s becomes fascinated with the glamorous lives of an aristocratic friend and his sister, but a complex love triangle develops between the three, while their idyllic relationship is threatened by the arrival of the siblings' embittered mother. Drama based on Evelyn Waugh's novel, starring Matthew Goode, Ben Whishaw, Hayley Atwell and Emma Thompson

programme.title: Face to Face
programme.desc: John Freeman faced a difficult subject in Evelyn Waugh when he interviewed him in 1960. Waugh, author of Brideshead Revisited, was in characteristically obstructive frame of mind. The result is a rare glimpse into the life and temperament of one of the greatest novelists of the 20th century

programme.title: African Renaissance: When Art Meets Power
programme.desc: Afua Hirsch explores the art, music and culture of Senegal, discovering a country with a cultural influence far beyond its size, with dynamic film, fashion and hip-hop scenes

programme.title: Handmade in Africa
programme.desc: Seydou Kane crafts a new kora from scratch in his studio in downtown Dakar, Senegal, an instrument also known as a West African lute or harp that dates back to the 13th century

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross creates a painting of a large old mill in the heart of a forest

programme.title: Johnny Kingdom - A Year on Exmoor
programme.desc: Spring proves a busy time for the amateur cameraman, but his mind is elsewhere as his pet deer Bambi falls sick. Johnny rescued the three-legged animal 12 years ago, but her illness leaves him facing a difficult decision

programme.title: The Beauty of Maps
programme.desc: The series heads for the British Library to look at maps of London, examining how representations of the city have changed over the course of three centuries. Among the documents examined are maps of the capital after the devastating fire of 1666, a neo-Classical interpretation of the city from 1746, and a map depicting sites of personal significance produced by contemporary cartographer Stephen Walter

programme.title: Girl Friday
programme.desc: Another chance to see this documentary following Absolutely Fabulous star Joanna Lumley as she attempts to survive on a desert island off Madagascar with only a few basic tools to help her

programme.title: Johnny Kingdom - A Year on Exmoor
programme.desc: With spring well under way, the amateur cameraman should be filming badgers, but there is no sign of any cubs and the food he has put down is being eaten by squirrels, pheasants and rodents. Johnny invites Tony Thorpe to the hide, hoping he will bring luck - but forgets about his aversion to rats

programme.title: Sounds of the Sixties
programme.desc: The late 1960s saw the first regular transmission of colour television, and the music world followed the trend with the launch of BBC television show Colour Me Pop. This episode features some of the programme's highlights, including performances by the Who, the Kinks, the Small Faces, the Bonzo Dog Doo-Dah Band and the Jimi Hendrix Experience

programme.title: Big Hits - Top of the Pops 1964 to 1975
programme.desc: Memorable archive performances from 1964 through to 1975, representing a sample of the first 12 years of Top of the Pops. Among those featured are the Rolling Stones, Tom Jones, Status Quo, Procol Harum, Stevie Wonder, Queen, the Kinks, Stealers Wheel, Julie Driscoll, Peter Sarstedt and the Seekers

programme.title: Peter Green: Man of the World
programme.desc: The story of the blues performer, who found fame in the 1960s as lead guitarist with Fleetwood Mac, but left the public stage for years as he battled drug addiction and mental illness. Featuring archive footage and interviews with Noel Gallagher, Carlos Santana, Green's former bandmates and the musician himself

programme.title: Fleetwood Mac - Don't Stop
programme.desc: Documentary looking back over the band's long career, from the early blues outfit led by Peter Green in the late 1960s, to their reincarnation in the 1970s with the line-up that went on to record the 40-million-selling album Rumours. Mick Fleetwood, John McVie, Lindsey Buckingham and Stevie Nicks reveal the truth behind the successes, failures and relationships that characterised their music and made them one of the most enduring acts in popular music

programme.title: Foo Fighters at Reading 2019
programme.desc: Dave Grohl, Taylor Hawkins, Nate Mendel, Chris Shiflett, Pat Smear and Rami Jaffee bring their rock marathon master class back to headlining Reading, a tradition they began in 2002. Huw Stephens introduces highlights from the set, as the band run through classic hits from their back catalogue

programme.title: Peter Green: Man of the World
programme.desc: The story of the blues performer, who found fame in the 1960s as lead guitarist with Fleetwood Mac, but left the public stage for years as he battled drug addiction and mental illness. Featuring archive footage and interviews with Noel Gallagher, Carlos Santana, Green's former bandmates and the musician himself

programme.title: The Real Jamie Johnson

Seb is an exciting young player, excelling at the Crystal Palace academy, who's had his fair share of knockbacks. Jermaine Johnson heads down to south London to watch him in action and find how early disappointments have spurred him on, and why he's looking forward to playing in an 11-a-side team for the first time.

programme.title: My Life
programme.desc: With just 2 years' experience, Scottish teenager Iona has only five months to prepare for the biggest international competition of her life, the World Junior Surf Championships in the Azores, Portugal. It's a race against time as she trains in sub-zero conditions and battles the unpredictable seas of her home shores to find the perfect wave. It's punishing but day after day Iona dons her wetsuit and dives in. But surfing off Scotland's coast is a world away from the warm, volcanic waters of the Azores, where Iona will be up against the gnarliest (treacherous) waves she has ever faced as well as some of the most experienced and fearless surfers on the planet. Determined not to let herself or her country down, will Iona master the surf - and the pressure - to come home a champion?

programme.title: Mr Stink (2012)
programme.desc: Family comedy based on the children's novel by David Walliams. Twelve-year-old Chloe leads a lonely existence - her mother is too busy with her political ambitions to take any interest in her daughter, her hen-pecked dad is hiding a secret of his own and she cannot hope to compete with her 'perfect' younger sister - to say nothing of the nasty girls who make her school life a misery. So when she meets a tramp in need of somewhere to stay, she invites him to hide in her family's garden shed - but it soon turns out there is more to her smelly guest than meets the eye. programme.desc: Giovanna and Baby Bear welcome Bella, Olive, Jack, Isobel, Jonas, Leo to The Baby Club.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Duggee is having a lovely cup of tea in his favourite cup when Roly surprises him with a very loud 'Peek-a-Boo', which is so loud that Duggee jumps and drops his cup which shatters into pieces

programme.title: Let's Play
programme.desc: It is Sid's turn for an industrious adventure as he steps through the magic curtains and becomes a carpenter

programme.title: Pablo
programme.desc: The little boy worries about the lift at the department store, but with the help of his friends in the Art World he learns what the buttons are for and how to get safely out again

programme.title: Our Family
programme.desc: Finlay and his family visit a big castle and look for fairies in the grounds. Later, it's all aboard a boat as they head out to sea on the lookout for seals

programme.title: Tinga Tinga Tales
programme.desc: Children's animation, based on a collection of African folk-tales, revealing why lizards hide under rocks

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Jake is making traditional Australian meat pies filled with lamb mince

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Evie, Doug, Jess and Gramps take Waffle to his first puppy training class

programme.title: Timmy Time
programme.desc: The youngster is crestfallen when he loses his home-made puppet on the way to nursery

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: The girl builds Duck a computer game as he is getting frustrated with his own

programme.title: Tee and Mo
programme.desc: The animated adventures of an energetic baby monkey and his long-suffering mother, who take a light-hearted approach to problem-solving and teamwork as they tackle life's challenges together

programme.title: Pablo
programme.desc: A little boy journeys from real-life into the animated art world he creates, where his friends help him handle situations that make him feel anxious

programme.title: Alphablocks
programme.desc: Fun and games with the letters C and K, which come to life, tell stories and make words

programme.title: Yakka Dee!
programme.desc: Dee encourages a friend to say ‘rocket' and we go on a whirlwind word adventure

programme.title: Numberblocks
programme.desc: Ten promises to take the others on a trip to the moon but blasts off on her own. Find out which pairs of numbers add to make 10 with the Numberblocks

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Flop teaches Bing and Pando how to blow bubbles, but frustration and outrage soon follow

programme.title: Twirlywoos
programme.desc: The Twirlywoos watch a farmer connect fence panels together to make a pen for his sheep. The sheep dog herds the sheep into the pen. Whilst the farmer is having a doze the Twirlywoos build an enclosure around him by connecting more fence panels together

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Educational programme for children aged between four and seven

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The lights suddenly go out in Home Dome, and the Teletubbies get the giggles. When they come back on, the colourful quartet discover they have made a mess everywhere

programme.title: Tinpo
programme.desc: Sailor Po can't sail her yacht unless Team Tinpo can find wind for her sails

programme.title: Olobob Top
programme.desc: While helping Bobble float his boat, Big Fish loses her 'pretty thing'. Flobbleobble sees the pair carried underwater on a magical journey through Big Fish's pond

programme.title: Pablo
programme.desc: Pablo loves the Book Animals, but when he gets a new book with a spider in it, Pablo thinks the spider looks scary. However, it turns out the spider is a bit frightened as well

programme.title: What's the Big Idea?
programme.desc: Hugo learns that an ant is incredibly strong for its size during a strength contest

programme.title: Time for School
programme.desc: It's raining hard, but the children put on their waterproofs and have a good time outside.

programme.title: Swashbuckle
programme.desc: Line is stuck inside a barrel and can't get out. Can anyone help him?

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: A big box is delivered to the Clubhouse, full of bits and pieces. Duggee and the squirrels are going to have to work very hard to put it all together

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.desc: The mischievous rabbit has to babysit Cotton-tale, but cannot resist going to an exciting event at the Squirrel camp and brings along his sleeping sister

programme.title: Love Monster
programme.desc: Love Monster wants to wear his favourite hat on Favourite Hat Day, but it's falling apart!

programme.title: Andy's Prehistoric Adventures
programme.desc: Andy Day travels back three million years where he tracks down the ancient tribe of australopithecus primates, but he has his trusty backpack stolen by hungry vultures

programme.title: Maddie's Do You Know?
programme.desc: Maddie uses a special camera to find out how the chain on a bicycle works

programme.title: Bitz & Bob
programme.desc: A race across the desert slows to a crawl when Bitz and Purl over-engineer their vehicle

programme.title: Go Jetters

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog

programme.title: JoJo & Gran Gran

programme.title: Moon and Me
programme.desc: A nursery rhyme book inspires Mr Onion to play at being Humpty Dumpty.

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: Small finds some 'space treasure' but doesn't know what it is for. Then Tiny too finds her own space treasure - a strange box. Only when they both realise that the two pieces might fit together, do they get to see the unexpectedly beautiful results

programme.title: In the Night Garden

programme.title: Escape to the Country

While there, the presenter heads to the seaside town of Worthing, and finds out how one of the oldest working cinemas in the country has been saved from redevelopment

programme.title: Call That Hard Work?

programme.title: Our Lives

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The team restores a rusty toy pedal car that is missing its steering wheel, a Victorian stamp perforating machine, a bronze statue of a dagger-wielding assassin by leading French artist Claire Colinet, and a baize-covered card table dating from the 1940s that has sentimental value for its owner

programme.title: Reported Missing
programme.desc: When his father misses an appointment with his mental health worker, 25-year-old Oli calls Gloucestershire Police, concerned about the whereabouts of his father, Michael, who suffers from paranoid schizophrenia and is thought to have stopped taking his medication. With no sign of Michael overnight, police launch an appeal for information, and CCTV searches of the town reveal that Michael got into a taxi two nights before and was taken to a high point in the Cotswold's. Officers prepare for a major search operation, but looking for someone hiding in a heavily wooded area presents them with major challenges

programme.title: Fort Salem
programme.desc: Supernatural drama set in an alternate America, following three young witches from basic training in combat magic into early deployment on the military frontline to fight against a dangerous rebel group called The Spree. programme.title: Fort Salem
programme.desc: The unit travels to Salem Town for an annual witch celebration, but an unexpected disturbance throws the proceedings into chaos. Meanwhile, Tally wonders if she made the right choice to embark on a military career. Supernatural drama, starring Taylor Hickson and Jessica Sutton

programme.title: Iolo: The Last Wilderness of Wales
programme.desc: Iolo Williams searches for a golden eagle. The bird became extinct in Wales 400 years ago, but an escaped golden eagle has been living in the mountains for 10 years

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: John Torode and Gregg Wallace recall some of the best - and the worst - reviews of the celebrities' creations. John and Gregg also revisit a classic challenge from 2011, in which the five semi-finalists were tasked with cooking the much-loved recipes from residents of a retirement village

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: The president of the local cricket club is found shot dead in the middle of the pitch after a recent victory. programme.title: Fighting the Power: Britain After George Floyd
programme.desc: Daniel Henry follows the Black Lives Matter campaign in the UK, as the country is forced to look again at its record on race in the wake of the death of George Floyd in the US

programme.title: Reported Missing

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Presented by Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Ben Fogle observes a Cuban crocodile death roll for the first time, while the keepers face a challenge from a pride of lairy lions and Jean Johansson meets some chatty parrots

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Properties in West Bromwich, Leeds and Croydon, all bought without viewing ahead of the auction, are sold under the hammer, and the owners reveal whether they regret their buys

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: A 95-year-old navy veteran is winched from the deck of a cruise ship, having lain undiscovered in his cabin for 11 hours. Plus, the dental team has to take to the road to get to patients across Shetland

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles is at the helm with experts Danny Sebastian and Caroline Hawley, as two teams scour the antiques centre in the hope that they can make the greater profit at auction. There are plenty of highs and lows in the saleroom, while Eric also gets the chance to meet an unlikely war hero

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: Kam is schooled in privilege, and Leonie makes excuses to stick with Erika. Fiona Press and Mitchell Bourke star

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: Consumer journalists Jasmine Birtles, Chris Hollins, Simon Calder, Adam Pearson and Rebecca Wilcox compete in a charity edition hosted by Jeremy Vine

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton is tasked with helping a couple and their young sons quit London to make a new life for themselves in Derbyshire, with a budget of £475,000. He also spends time with an artisan craftsman who is bringing back the local skill of knife-making

programme.title: Call That Hard Work?
programme.desc: Poultry farmer Andrew, restaurant manager Angela and performer Jack go head to head in a bid to prove their job is the hardest. The trio get off to a cracking start sorting thousands of eggs on poultry farmer Andrew's smallholding in Berkshire. While performer Jack takes his new role a little too slowly, restaurant manager Angela finds herself in a total scramble. The next day, both Andrew and Jack struggle to memorise four menus as they prepare to serve 40 covers at the high-end Indian restaurant where Angela works. For their final day, both the poultry farmer and restaurant manager face their fears to perform in front of a 45-strong audience at top tourist attraction the London Dungeon. Narrated by Sunetra Sarker

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Philip Serrell and Stephanie Connell embark on the last leg of their journey. Steph tries her hand at playing the bagpipes in Hexham, Northumberland, while Phil finds out how coal mining led to the development of Britain's rail network. The duo then meet at the final auction in Darlington, Co Durham

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Ronan Keating acts as co-host alongside Alex Jones for the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: Wallace & Gromit: A Matter of Loaf and Death
programme.desc: The duo open their own bakery, Top Bun, and business is soon booming - not least because a 'cereal killer' is targeting all the rival bakers in town. Even better, Wallace has caught the eye of local beauty Piella Bakewell. But Gromit is more concerned that his master might become the killer's next victim. With the voices of Peter Sallis and Sally Lindsay

programme.title: The Royal Edinburgh Military Tattoo
programme.desc: Bill Paterson narrates highlights of the event held on the esplanade of Edinburgh Castle, with the RAF College Band taking centre stage as the year's home service. Others featured include the Combined Bands of the Royal Cavalry of the Sultanate of Oman, the United States Air Force Honor Guard Drill Team, the Royal Marines Band Plymouth, the Band of the Royal Regiment of Scotland, the Central Band of the Czech Armed Forces and the Banda Monumental de Mexico, the Tattoo Dance Company and the Top Secret Drum Corps

programme.title: Tudur's TV Flashback
programme.desc: Comedian Tudur Owen rewinds the funniest, most fascinating clips from half a century and more of Welsh television. This time he's pushing back the boundaries of science and discovering a world of arcade games, bionic councillors and Welsh rocket men, full of lively characters from the nation's TV past

programme.title: Rhod Gilbert's Work Experience
programme.desc: The comedian undertakes a work placement at the Welsh Mountain Zoo near Colwyn Bay, Conwy, where his tasks include looking after tigers, sea lions and monkeys. However, Rhod struggles to remember the exotic creatures' names, let alone how to take care of them

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Conor and Jock decide it's time to move out of home and head to Spain to run a bar. First to receive the Spain news is Mairead, who is in disbelief - how is she going to keep digging them out of holes when they're living overseas? Billy Murphy receives unexpected information that causes him to faint - resulting in a large lump on his head and concern for Mairead and the lads. Then, Sergeant Healy arrives at the door with a barbershop quartet serenading Mairead as he's about to propose. Chris Walley, Alex Murphy and Hilary Rose star

programme.title: The Witches of Eastwick (1987)
programme.desc: Three single women dabble in magic to conjure up the perfect lover, and unintentionally summon Satan himself, who seduces them one by one. They come to realise the danger the lecherous devil presents and decide to end their relationships, but the infernal rogue does not take kindly to rejection. Fantasy comedy based on John Updike's novel, with Jack Nicholson, Cher, Michelle Pfeiffer and Susan Sarandon
