But what will the customers think of the changes?

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Chef Gordon Ramsay heads to Charlie's Italian bistro in La Verne, southern California, where the owner's lack of business nous is putting her relatives' homes at risk, after her mother and sister put them up as collateral. To make matters worse, the restaurant's food is less than appetising and its long-serving chef seems to be incompetent

programme.title: Inside Missguided: Made in Manchester
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes at one of Britain's biggest online fashion retailer's offices to explore what life is like for their young, female-dominated workforce at their offices in the heart of Manchester. The dresses team race against time to design a new range and hit their sales target for party season. Meanwhile, the ethics team are busy securing a new supplier in Leicester, and it is essential to get things right due to fast fashion's ethics being in the spotlight

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In west London, Daniela and Stuart kick off the culinary competition by serving an Italian menu, but they soon discover Manisha, with husband Akshay in tow, is hard to please. Next, young couple Josh and Georgie stake their claim to the £1,000 prize, planning a night of high quality food and a party atmosphere, complete with a private party bus. Night three sees Manisha serve a fusion menu of Indian and Western food, but the guests are more entertained by her wedding photos

programme.title: This Is Us
programme.desc: Jack and Rebecca make things difficult for themselves when they agree to throw Kate, Kevin and Randall three separate parties for their tenth birthday. While Kevin makes a major decision about his romantic life, Randall struggles to find time for William as things intensify at the office, and Kate's gastric bypass journey takes an unexpected turn. Starring Mandy Moore, Milo Ventimiglia and Sterling K Brown

programme.title: How to Avoid a Second Wave
programme.desc: Doctors Xand van Tulleken and Guddi Singh examine the possibility of a second wave of Covid-19 in the UK,in light of rising infection rates, local lockdowns and the return of children to schools. The programme examines what can be done to avoid a second wave, how best for people to prepare themselves, and if the NHS and the UK's test and trace system are equipped to deal with the possibility

programme.desc: The celebrities panic when the day of their mock tests arrives. Tattoo Fixers' Sketch gets really depressed when he finds out he's failed and threatens not to turn up to the real test. programme.desc: Howard's fear of commitment causes him to avoid Bernadette, but he soon realises he has made a mistake and tries to compensate for it. programme.desc: Bernadette asks Leonard about his physics experiment, making Howard so jealous that she threatens to end their relationship. Penny also feels put out by the situation, and asks Sheldon to teach her about science. Johnny Galecki, Simon Helberg and Melissa Rauch star

programme.desc: Jim Rogers, chairman and CEO of Kampgrounds of America, goes undercover within his own company to discover whether his employees share his boy-scout philosophy. programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The chef travels to La Grange, a suburb of Chicago, to help the owners of Cafe 36, an upmarket bistro threatening to destroy the marriage of its owners due to the scale of their financial losses. The head chef's attitude to quality control is driving customers away, and it is up to Gordon Ramsay to turn the business around

programme.title: River Cottage
programme.desc: Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall turns his attention to vegetables. He also takes a look at ways to prepare broad beans and celery, while Gill Meller demonstrates how to use potato as a main course, and Pam Corbin makes piccalilli

Scarlette hopes to make a splash with the five options she lines up for their £140,000 budget

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: As the B&B owners meet for the final time, things get heated when Martin and Kyle want to know why Jean and Dec gave their dressing gowns a thumbs down. Mary and Julian are keen to understand why the price wasn't right for Jean and Dec, while Kerry and Andy want to know why they got a low hosting score

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Bristol, the fifth and final cook to host is 50-year-old retiree and lady of leisure Shelley, who's hoping to impress with her tried and tested favourites. She's been talking about her holidays and lifestyle throughout the week, but will ex-pub landlady Christine have to call time on the chat tonight? She will also have to win over checkout assistant Jamie-Lea, hummus entrepreneur James, and pig farmer Chris

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Strong men are left quaking by the news that alcohol has been made illegal in Springfield. Luckily salvation is at hand in the form of prohibition-busting bootlegger Homer, who brews his own beer to meet demand. Featuring the guest voice of Joe Mantegna

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: When a modernised Springfield Elementary School has a technical meltdown, Lisa does her best to transform it into a different institution. During the process, Groundskeeper Willie coaches her and the other members of the Mathlympics team for an important maths quiz against their Waverly Hill rivals

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: Unreported World
programme.desc: Filmed before lockdown, Marcel Theroux reports from Thailand on the controversial Dhammakaya movement, which has millions of followers around the world. Dhammakaya claims its a force for good - updating Buddhism for the modern world, but critics say it's a money-obsessed cult. The movement believes in reincarnation and there's a big focus on making donations to pave the way to Nirvana, with offering boxes and cashpoints enabling the faithful to donate. Its founder, Abbot Dhammajayo, is in hiding following allegations that the sect has laundered £25million of stolen money, which he denies

programme.title: Lodgers for Codgers
programme.desc: The chances of a 25 to 35-year-old person owning a home have halved in the last 20 years, while millions of over-sixties are sitting on empty rooms, so here young and old tackle Britain's housing crisis by trialling a unique solution - moving in together. In this first episode, 19-year-old manager Liam moves in with 83-year-old straight-talker Flo in Brighton, while Londoner Nicole heads to Hastings to live with Claudine and Ted. How will Liam cope with his first taste of independence, what will Flo make of a night out on the town, and can Claudine and Ted succeed in showing Nicole a more affordable way to live?

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: A compilation of the best bits from recent series with host Jimmy Carr, team captains Sean Lock and Jon Richardson, and Countdown regulars Susie Dent and Rachel Riley, joined by a host of top comedians including Alan Carr, Richard Ayoade, Roisin Conaty, Aisling Bea, Nick Mohammed, Joe Wilkinson, Sara Pascoe, Kevin Bridges and Joe Lycett

programme.title: Ghostbusters (2016)
programme.desc: When a series of supernatural disturbances occurs in New York City, two experts in the paranormal investigate, joined by a brilliant inventor who has created an array of ghost-hunting gadgets and a public transport worker whose knowledge of the city provides vital clues to the sinister cause of the ghostly activity. Reboot of the 1980s comedy franchise, starring Kristen Wiig, Melissa McCarthy, Kate McKinnon, Leslie Jones and Chris Hemsworth

programme.title: Project Almanac (2015)

programme.title: Walking Britain's Lost Railways
programme.desc: Rob Bell explores the Waverley Route, which ran south from Edinburgh for 100 miles through the Scottish Borders to Carlisle, connecting with what is now known as the West Coast Main Line. After years of campaigning, the first stage of Rob's journey is aboard the new Borders Railway, the longest railway to open in over a century. It follows part of the old Waverley Route, making fine use of the 170-year-old, 23-arch Newbattle Viaduct. Rob also pays a visit to one of Hawick's surviving cashmere factories and discovers a First World War prisoner of war camp served by the railway

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: Derbyshire officers target violent gangs ram-raiding cash machines, with criminals using hydraulic power cutters and saws to cut the ATMs out, gas pumped into the cash slots to blow them up, or heavy construction vehicles to smash them out of the walls. Meanwhile, Sgt Adrian is called to a report of an attempted burglary and Vicky is alerted to a suspected stolen car with fake plates on the M1

programme.title: Police Code Zero: Officer Under Attack
programme.desc: Programme exploring the rising number of assaults on police officers, revealing what they face in the line of duty, and the emotional toll these incidents take. A late-night patrol pulls over a man suspected of driving under the influence, but they soon find themselves dealing with much more. Meanwhile, officers responded to an emergency call by residents on an estate, but the atmosphere takes a worrying turn when the residents turn on the officers

programme.title: Cannibals & Killers: Inside the World's Toughest Prisons
programme.desc: Documentary offering a never-before seen look inside three high-security Russian prisons. Cameras go inside Europe's oldest operating jail, Vladimir Central, Russia's largest prison with more than 1,200 inmates, and one of its convicts opens up about killing his brother-in-law for disturbing his daughter's sleep. Cameras also explore the Black Dolphin prison, near the Kazakhstan border, which is home to some of the highest category offenders in the world, and a frozen Siberian prison camp, where two inmates go their separate ways as one gets parole, while the other does not

programme.title: The Sex Business
programme.desc: Documentary taking a look at how people buy, sell and market sex. A disparate group of relatively new recruits to Britain's flourishing and fast-growing porn industry reveals what life is really like when trying to make a living in the adult movie industry

programme.title: Good Girls' Guide to Kinky Sex
programme.desc: The bedroom experiment continues as three self-confessed good girls and their guys look for creative ways to improve their sex lives. Finally they try a little role play by acting out their partner's ultimate fantasy for the night

Behind the front door, they find a critically ill two-year-old child with major heart problems, who is on constant life support and unable to breathe without a ventilator. Meanwhile, Brian O'Shaughnessy and Del Anglin chase £2,000 worth of debt owed by a business based in Kent, and recover £4,000 from a recycling business in Buckinghamshire. Plus, Stewart and Elmore encounter another family in crisis in Birmingham, where a retired couple who acted as guarantor for their daughter's tenancy are liable to pay over £3,000 after she defaulted

programme.title: The Railways That Built Britain with Chris Tarrant
programme.desc: Chris examines the devastating impact of the Beeching report, which led to half the country's stations and almost a third of the lines being closed. He also looks at how the railways were reinvented for the modern age, and how a sleek new locomotive not only saved the railway network, but reshaped the country

programme.title: Chris Tarrant: Extreme Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Chris travels along the route of the Soviet-era Trans-Caucasus Railway, beginning in the Azerbaijani capital of Baku. He then heads into Georgia, exploring Tbilisi's vintage metro system and funicular railway, before travelling to the holiday resort of Borjomi, where world-class engineering is on display in a mountain rail bridge designed by Gustave Eiffel

programme.title: GPs: Behind Closed Doors
programme.desc: An edition looking at the risks associated with being a frontline NHS worker, including a paramedic who is concerned about a hoarse voice he has had for several months, and is worried it could be signs of HIV infection after a patient assaulted him and punctured his skin. Elsewhere, Dr Arif's first patient Mohammed has a painful abscess the size of an apple on his backside

programme.title: Hoarders
programme.desc: Deep-cleaning expert George and his team face one of their toughest assignments yet - a house in Crosby, Merseyside that is almost literally bursting at the seams with clutter. Owner Paul has decided enough is enough, and he wants to sell his house and hoard to fund his retirement. But the property is infested with vermin and the kitchen a biohazard, so it is not going to be an easy sell in its current state

programme.title: Shopping for a New Penis
programme.desc: Documentary following the story of theatre performer Richard from London, whose entire penis was surgically removed due to an aggressive form of penile cancer. Richard is still coming to terms with the physical and emotional impact of this ordeal on his everyday life and relationships, but with a candid sense of humour, he is confronting his situation head-on - sharing his story as a warning to others about a disease on the increase

programme.title: Secrets of the Luxury Super Yachts

programme.title: The Gadget Show
programme.desc: As the programme bows out for the summer Craig Charles, Jon Bentley Georgie Barrat and Ortis Deley take a shedload of the latest tech for a camping adventure. From the techiest water pistols, the smartest campsite imaginable to a rundown of the must-have off road bikes, the gang has all the kit conceivably required for the season. On top of all that, Jon busts a move at the campsite party hosted by Craig, acting as DJ. Plus, a new feature sees Craig attempt to turn a house into a smart home using so-called 'easy fit' DIY kits

programme.title: Inside Tiffany's: World's Most Famous Jewellers
programme.desc: A look at the iconic jewellery brand, founded in New York in 1837, which has harnessed the power of royalty and celebrity to appeal to fashionistas and lovers of beautiful jewellery design. Company insiders and industry experts reveal the story of how Tiffany and Co went from a small American trinket store to a multibillion-pound company - and an international icon of style, bold artistry and innovative design. Through fascinating archive and insightful interviews, this documentary celebrates the brand, which reached iconic status thanks to Audrey Hepburn's film Breakfast at Tiffany's

programme.title: We LOVE Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: Celebrity fans including Fern Britton, the Rev Richard Coles, Laurence Llewelyn-Bowen, Patsy Palmer, Sherrie Hewson, Christopher Biggins and Amanda Barrie join in this celebration of David Croft and Jeremy Lloyd's department store-set sitcom, which ran for 70 episodes from 1972 to 1985. Plus, family and friends of Mollie Sugden, John Inman, Trevor Bannister, Frank Thornton and Wendy Richard share their personal memories and never-before-seen private photographs of the cast

programme.title: Are You Being Served? (1977)
programme.desc: Havoc descends on the coast of Spain as the motley Grace Brothers crew arrive for their annual holiday on the Costa Plonka, which does not exactly live up to the brochure's promise of rest and relaxation. programme.title: House Doctor
programme.desc: Ann Maurice tackles a period property in Bow, in the heart of London's East End, hoping to attract potential buyers by clearing clutter and creating an elegant dining area. Among other improvements, she ditches a navy blue bathroom suite in favour of one in stylish white to ensure the house will fetch the asking price of £300,000

programme.title: House Doctor
programme.desc: Ann Maurice visits Headcorn to help a couple spruce up their tiny cottage in a bid to make it more attractive to prospective buyers, clearing out all their clutter, getting rid of a dingy carpet and using small furniture to create an illusion of space

programme.title: Over the Hedge (2006)
programme.desc: A raccoon trickster is fretting about his debt to a fearsome bear when he stumbles on some animals waking in the woods after their winter nap. Spotting the chance to solve his problem, the raccoon dupes the gullible creatures into stealing food from a nearby suburb so he can give it to the bear - but they fail to realise they will soon be at the mercy of a vermin exterminator. Animated comedy, with the voices of Bruce Willis, Steve Carell, Garry Shandling and William Shatner

programme.title: Mary and the Witch's Flower (2017)
programme.desc: A strange flower grants a girl magic powers. Animated adventure based on The Little Broomstick by Mary Stewart, featuring the voices of Kate Winslet and Jim Broadbent. Dubbed in English

programme.title: Teen Wolf (1985)
programme.desc: A teenage misfit is initially dismayed to learn he is a werewolf as a result of a hereditary curse. He finds there are advantages as he becomes a local celebrity, while his enhanced agility makes him the star player of his school's basketball team - but fame quickly goes to his head. Comedy, starring Michael J Fox, James Hampton and Susan Ursitti

programme.title: Chasing Mavericks (2012)
programme.desc: A teenager discovers a supposedly legendary stretch of shoreline famous for vast waves actually exists. He tracks down a famous surfer and asks for help in developing his skills to the point where he can successfully ride the challenging waters. Fact-based drama, starring Jonny Weston, Gerard Butler and Elisabeth Shue

programme.title: Night at the Museum: Secret of the Tomb (2014)
programme.desc: Nightwatchman Larry notices that the living exhibits of his museum are behaving strangely. He discovers that the magical artefact that brings them to life is damaged, so he and his friends travel to London's Natural History Museum to find a way to restore it. Fantasy comedy sequel, starring Ben Stiller, Rebel Wilson and Robin Williams in his final film role

programme.title: Den of Thieves (2018)
programme.desc: An elite unit of the Los Angeles county sheriffs' department investigates a series of perfectly executed heists, and uncovers a criminal gang composed of military veterans, whose strategic and combat experience make them an elusive foe. The investigators set out to bring them to justice before they can carry out their biggest robbery yet - stealing £120million from the Federal Reserve Bank. Crime drama, starring Gerard Butler and Jordan Bridges

programme.title: Stratton (2017)
programme.desc: A British Special Boat Service commando tracks down an international terrorist cell. Thriller, starring Dominic Cooper, Austin Stowell and Gemma Chan

programme.title: Una (2016)
programme.desc: A young woman arrives unexpectedly at an older man's workplace looking for the truth behind the affair that occurred between them. Drama, starring Rooney Mara, Ben Mendelsohn and Riz Ahmed

programme.title: FernGully: The Last Rainforest (1992)
programme.desc: A fairy living in a forest encounters a member of a lumber crew and magically shrinks him down to her size. He learns about the damage humans are doing to the area, and helps defend it when his fellow loggers unwittingly unleash an evil spirit that had been entombed in a tree. Animated adventure, with the voices of Robin Williams, Christian Slater and Tim Curry

programme.title: Rise of the Guardians (2012)
programme.desc: Magical beings from folklore, including Santa Claus, Jack Frost and the Easter Bunny, join forces to defeat the plans of an evil spirit, who intends to steal the dreams of children and use the power they grant him to dominate the world. Animated adventure, with the voices of Chris Pine, Alec Baldwin, Hugh Jackman, Isla Fisher and Jude Law

programme.title: Wonder (2017)
programme.desc: A 10-year-old boy with facial disfigurements that have so far required almost 30 operations takes on a whole new challenge when, after being home educated his entire life, enters mainstream school for the first time. As he struggles to fit in, his fellow pupils and the community are forced to learn the power of compassion and acceptance. Julia Roberts, Owen Wilson and Jacob Tremblay star in Stephen Chbosky's drama, based on the best-selling novel by RJ Palacio

programme.title: Crocodile Dundee II (1988)
programme.desc: The rugged Australian adventurer sets out to save his reporter girlfriend from New York drug dealers. Having rescued her from their clutches, he realises they will soon be coming after him - so he decides to even the odds by luring them to the Outback for a final showdown. Comedy adventure sequel, starring Paul Hogan, Linda Kozlowski, John Meillon and Charles S Dutton

programme.title: The Day the Earth Stood Still (2008)
programme.desc: An alien arrives on Earth, accompanied by an awesomely powerful robot, on a fateful mission - to decide if the human race deserves to continue - and if not, to wipe it out. The visitor is imprisoned in a military facility, but a scientist helps him escape, hoping to prove that mankind is worth saving. Sci-fi drama remake, starring Keanu Reeves and Jennifer Connelly

programme.title: Get Out (2017)
programme.desc: After African American photographer Chris and his white girlfriend Rose visit her wealthy parents, he suspects there's more to the locals than meets the eye. Oscar-winning psychological thriller written and directed by Jordan Peele (Us), starring Daniel Kaluuya, Allison Williams, Bradley Whitford and Caleb Landry Jones

programme.title: Candyman (1992)
programme.desc: A graduate student is researching urban legends for her dissertation and investigates the tale of a supernatural, hook-handed serial killer rumoured to haunt a run-down Chicago apartment block. Though cynical at first, she soon finds herself face to face with the murderous soul, who sets his sights on claiming her as his latest victim. Horror, starring Virginia Madsen, Tony Todd and Xander Berkeley

programme.title: Racer and the Jailbird (2017)
programme.desc: Gigi, a high-flying gangster in Brussels, falls for Bibi, a young racing driver with upper-class roots. Thriller, starring Matthias Schoenaerts, Adele Exarchopoulos and Eric De Staercke. In French and Flemish

programme.title: Harry and the Hendersons (1987)
programme.desc: A hapless family's camping holiday in the mountains is brought to a sudden end when they accidentally hit the legendary Bigfoot with their car. They decide to adopt the endearing creature, but have more trouble than expected trying to keep him concealed and to avoid arousing their neighbours' suspicions. Comedy, starring John Lithgow, Melinda Dillon, David Suchet, Don Ameche and Kevin Peter Hall

programme.title: Stuart Little 2 (2002)
programme.desc: The plucky talking mouse feels left out when the son of his adopted human family makes a new pal - but quickly forgets such trifling matters when his high-flying canary friend is captured by an evil falcon, prompting the brave rodent to mount a daring rescue. Family adventure sequel, with Geena Davis, Hugh Laurie and Jonathan Lipnicki, and the voices of Michael J Fox, Melanie Griffith and James Woods

programme.title: Rio 2 (2014)
programme.desc: Parrots Jewel and Blu lead a happy life together in Rio with their three chicks, until they find themselves hurled into an expedition up the Amazon. The journey leads to an encounter with an old enemy, while Blu also has to win the approval of Jewel's fearsome father. Animated comedy sequel, with the voices of Jesse Eisenberg, Anne Hathaway and Jamie Foxx

programme.title: The Return of the Pink Panther (1975)
programme.desc: The inept Inspector Clouseau sets out to unmask a notorious international jewel thief believed to be behind the theft of a priceless diamond - but anarchy, as ever, follows wherever he goes. Blake Edwards' comedy sequel, starring Peter Sellers, Christopher Plummer, Catherine Schell, Herbert Lom, Burt Kwouk and Graham Stark

programme.title: Daddy's Home (2015)
programme.desc: A mild-mannered man hopes for his wife's two children to view him as their father. His dreams of perfect family life are threatened by the return of their biological father, a charismatic, motorcycle-riding tough guy, and he pulls out all the stops to prove to his stepchildren that he can be just as cool. Comedy, starring Will Ferrell and Mark Wahlberg

programme.title: Finding Your Feet (2017)
programme.desc: A wealthy woman's privileged existence is shattered when she discovers her husband is having an affair with her best friend. Seeking solace, she turns to her estranged sister, who lives in a London council estate, and finds a new lease of life in joining her community dance class. Comedy, starring Imelda Staunton, Celia Imrie, Timothy Spall and Joanna Lumley

programme.title: They Live (1988)
programme.desc: A drifter arrives in Los Angeles seeking work, only to discover the city's population is being dominated by aliens who control their victims through subliminal advertising. John Carpenter's satirical sci-fi adventure, starring Roddy Piper, Keith David and Meg Foster

programme.title: Sorcerer (1977)
programme.desc: Four men with shady pasts take a lucrative and potentially fatal delivery job driving two trucks full of dangerously unstable dynamite along equally unsound roads through the South American jungle. programme.desc: Erica decides to put college on hold and pursue her dream of becoming a pop singer - which causes her parents no end of stress. programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

programme.desc: Tension mounts when the pals attend a weekend science conference. Howard becomes argumentative after Bernadette runs into a former boyfriend, and the fallout causes chaos with the hotel room arrangements. Rick Fox guest stars, with Simon Helberg and Melissa Rauch

programme.desc: Adam tries his hand at organising the school's big Thanksgiving feast alongside Don and Lowell, and Andi struggles not to intervene as the trio's plans threaten to derail the event

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Barry accidentally records over one of Beverly's prized family videotapes - her treasured keepsake from his fifth birthday party. Meanwhile, Adam and Murray build a robot together, but the project comes between them when they experience creative differences

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Adam goes to great lengths to dodge swimming classes, but his plan to avoid the sport could affect his friendship with Dave. Jealousy prompts Barry to undermine Erica when she attempts to make the world a better place by forming a school social awareness club

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Peralta is put in charge of Captain Holt's security after he receives several death threats - a responsibility he not only enjoys, but also abuses as much as he can. Santiago tries to encourage her colleagues to take a Christmas photo, only to be met with a lack of enthusiasm - and Diaz's refusal to smile. US cop comedy, starring Andy Samberg

programme.desc: After promising Davina he would leave Tracey to start a new relationship with her, Dean discovers a real connection between him and Tracey developing at the worst possible moment

programme.title: Celebrity Gogglebox

programme.title: Gogglebox

programme.title: Buffy the Vampire Slayer

Glory targets Buffy's family in her quest for the Key, while the slayer discovers another faction is in town seeking to destroy it rather than allow it to fall into the wrong hands

programme.title: Buffy the Vampire Slayer
programme.desc: Buffy decides her friends have to be told what Dawn really is if they are to risk their lives protecting her. When Dawn breaks into the magic shop at night, she learns the truth herself. programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon becomes concerned that his comfortable lifestyle is having an impact on his professional progress and is stopping him from achieving scientific greatness, so he asks the gang to make life more difficult for him. programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: Fly-on-the-wall series turning the cameras on TV's most vocal armchair critics, as the show's regular gaggle of families, friends and viewing party acquaintances settle down to share their insightful, passionate and sometimes emotional critiques on what they have been watching, including World on Fire, Antiques Roadshow, Treasure Island with Bear Grylls, The Circle: Vote Night and Britain's Got Talent: The Champions

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: Interactive documentary in which singletons meet up with possible partners from the internet for the first time. This week's participants include Amanda, who is looking for her future husband, and recruitment consultant Siofra, whose search for someone with personality as well as looks turns up a male model who used to be a pilot. At the end of their restaurant meal, the couples decide whether they want to take things further, and if the answer is no, the next date sitting opposite them could be a viewer

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Beverly discovers Adam is failing in Spanish class, and immediately blames his teacher. However, when she arranges for him to receive extra tuition, her plan backfires

programme.title: The Goldbergs

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In East Sussex, the first hosts are childhood sweethearts Robin and Maria. Robin is the brains behind the cooking, but it's up to Maria to ensure the hosting is spot on. programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Jake meets an attractive medical examiner at a crime scene, but annoys his colleagues when his attempts to get together with her cause a hold-up with an autopsy report. Meanwhile, Amy discovers Jeffords has some hidden talents when the police sketch artist goes off sick

programme.title: Young Sheldon

programme.title: Buffy the Vampire Slayer

programme.desc: Meemaw meets Dale's ex-wife June, while Sheldon is forced to work on a group project with reluctant college students. Iain Armitage stars

Meanwhile, Teddy stops hanging out with the few friends he has in school to spend all of his free time with his online gaming pals, making his parents concerned

programme.title: The Goldbergs

programme.title: Young Sheldon

Co-starring Kevin Hart and Amy Ryan

programme.title: Buffy the Vampire Slayer
programme.desc: Buffy is devastated to return home one day and discover her mother has died - leaving the friends struggling to deal with a tragedy they are unable to do anything about. programme.title: Buffy the Vampire Slayer

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Dick Strawbridge fixes up his temperamental van at his chateau, while Erin and JB start work on their honeymoon suite and solarium/sun room

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: Charlie Luxton meets people as they bid to turn an empty plot of land into their dream home on an affordable budget. Adam and Vicki Nicholl have become tired of city life and decide to move back to the Hertfordshire village of Pirton where they grew up to build a barn-style property in the garden of Adam's mum's home. The pair are convinced they can save around £100,000 simply by not purchasing an existing property

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B competition heads to France as four British expats compete, beginning with Lisanne Ashton, who runs the rural Les Chimeres in the south of the country. After searching for truffles in the surrounding area, guest Tony Horton's passion for the royal family causes him to clash with fellow competitor Shaun Baggs, whose mood is worsened by the discovery of bugs in his bed

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition continues at Le Manoir in the Dordogne, owned by royalist Tony Horton and his Armenian wife Shou Shou - who make a poor first impression by presenting guests with a timetable specifying when they are allowed to use the bathroom. A vinegar-tasting session also receives a tepid response, and some of the visitors are bemused by their hosts turning up for breakfast in costume

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Wine-loving Shaun Baggs and his partner Patrick Weaver bring the contest to Le Clos Rabelais in central France, where they invite fellow competitors to take part in a traditional French dance class. Tension mounts as George and Linda become increasingly frustrated with Lisanne, and the bickering continues over dinner when Shaun is confronted about his previous negative feedback

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: George and Linda McGill welcome their guests to the Moulin de la Civiere near Rostrenen in Brittany, where entertainment comes in the shape of a tenpin-bowling outing, and the couple lay on a full English breakfast. However, the hosts receive an unexpected surprise at feedback time courtesy of Lisanne

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants meet one last time to learn how much they have been paid by their fellow hoteliers before discovering who has won the week's competition. Old tensions resurface as the results are announced, and there are heated exchanges around the table

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Orang-utan sisters Emma and Subis are due to give birth at the same time, but after being in labour for more than 24 hours with no progress, Subis is rushed to the vet for a possible C-section. Meerkats Beagle and Huskie have moved to the zoo as the new breeding pair, but their new housemates, the aardvarks, are making them anxious. In an effort to make them more comfortable in their new home, the team decide to redesign the paddock to give the meerkats their own space

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A boxer dog is brought in with extensive injuries to its pelvis and hind legs from a road accident. Pioneering surgeon Noel Fitzpatrick considers a stem-cell scaffolding procedure - but there are significant risks attached. He and his team also help different animals with broken legs, including hedgehogs, and a kitten that has had an accident at home

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Erin and JB host a 90th birthday party, and a family begin working on their Yoga retreat. Elsewhere, the day of the wedding party arrives for Tim and Margreeth

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud returns to Devon for an update on an inspirational build. Royal Marine captain Jon White and his wife Becky were living happily in a small cottage when in June 2010, while serving in Afghanistan, Jon stepped on an improvised explosive device, losing three limbs. Every part of their lives had to change - starting with where they could live. Their architect came up with a cutting-edge, semi open-plan building with a dramatic wing-like-roof - and crucially no obvious signs of adaptations for Jon's disabilities. Has the new home proved as enabling for the couple and their expanding family as they had hoped?

programme.title: Eye in the Sky (2015)
programme.desc: Colonel Katherine Powell's mission to wipe out a terrorist cell in Kenya is complicated when a girl enters the blast radius of the intended drone strike. Powell has to decide between sacrificing the girl's life or letting the terrorists flee to carry out more atrocities. Thriller, starring Helen Mirren, Alan Rickman and Aaron Paul

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: This edition sees staff at King's College Hospital in south London treat patients involved in serious road accidents. Former soldier Steve, 48, was found unconscious at the wheel, 11-year-old schoolgirl Scarlett has been knocked down by a car, and Pauline, 70, was hit by a vehicle that mounted the pavement, seriously injuring her lower legs

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy panel show's version of the words-and-numbers quiz, as Jon Richardson and David Mitchell take on guest captain Joe Wilkinson and Roisin Conaty. Tom Allen joins Countdown's resident lexicographer Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner and Rachel Riley looks after the numbers and letters

The pair are convinced they can save around £100,000 simply by not purchasing an existing property

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: This edition sees staff at King's College Hospital in south London treat patients involved in serious road accidents. Former soldier Steve, 48, was found unconscious at the wheel, 11-year-old schoolgirl Scarlett has been knocked down by a car, and Pauline, 70, was hit by a vehicle that mounted the pavement, seriously injuring her lower legs

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty visits Spain to meet an entrepreneur who is investing a large amount of money in an attempt to farm the critically endangered Atlantic bluefin tuna. With just one of these fish selling for a record £1million at auction in Tokyo, the rewards could be huge. Kate Quilton investigates why ice cream in freezers can sometimes get gritty ahead of the best before date, while Matt Tebbutt tries to find out what the difference is between the supermarkets' premium and budget range of frozen peas

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance for viewers to purchase goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance for viewers to purchase goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Kirstie's Vintage Gems
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp demonstrates how to create handmade and personalised gifts. She learns about sugarcraft and perfume-making, and also receives a lesson in the art of designing Victorian-style silhouettes

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: Irum and her husband Murtaza have a family nest egg to invest in a property, but need to decide whether they want to stay close to their Midlands base or relocate to the Costa Almeria in Spain. Jasmine Harman and Jonnie Irwin show the couple what they could buy in each location

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole tries to restore a monkey bike, and Simon O'Brien makes use of all his skills as he tries to give new life to an old racing bicycle

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Simon O'Brien finds a barn containing a collection of replica Rolls-Royce cars, and Henry Cole tries his hand at driving a tractor as they continue their money-making tour

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Erin and JB host a 90th birthday party, and a family begin working on their Yoga retreat. Elsewhere, the day of the wedding party arrives for Tim and Margreeth

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: Charlie Luxton helps Bill and Angie Jenkins, who plan to knock down their uneconomical bungalow on the bank of the River Thames and build a new home to fit in with the location. Potentially, the couple could have an investment profit of £330,000 by opting to self-build, and they visit a nearby property for inspiration on how to make their project more stylish

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Owner Helen Marriott offers her guest a chance to 'step off the planet' and treats them to essential breathing lessons, a chance to get to know one another in the hot tub, and soul-soothing foot massages at the Fountains Court Holistic Health Hotel in the North Yorkshire resort of Scarborough

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants head to the Whittington & Cat, a pub in Hull that also offers B&B. Proprietor Sue Perkins treats her guests to an afternoon of ice hockey, but they are intrigued by the possible contents of her peculiarly titled breakfast menu

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Khurram Saba hosts the third day of the competition at his Saba Hotel in west London and finds his guests are expecting metropolitan chic, and certainly not fish and chips for dinner. Candida Leaver is extremely displeased when she experiences a sleepless night, while Sue Perkins and Gill take the opportunity to go out and have some fun

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Candida Leaver and Peter Starling, owners of the Bower Inn in Bridgwater, Somerset, play host in the last round of the week. The group activity is willow-weaving, and at dinner time the guests reveal what they think of their no-nonsense hosts

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants meet one last time to learn how much they have been paid by their fellow hoteliers before discovering who has won the week's competition. Old tensions resurface as the results are announced, and there are heated exchanges around the table

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: African painted dogs K'mana and Ville were thrown together as a breeding couple after the dominant female died, but K'mana was never Ville's first choice as partner and still carries a limp from her battles with Ville's previous mate. Now she is pregnant with the zoo's first ever litter of this rare and endangered species. Meanwhile, keepers try to rekindle interest between Okapi pair Dicky and Stuma, and Atlas moths Dave and Simon only have five days of life after hatching, and their sole purpose is to mate

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Documentary following the work of pioneering veterinary practice Fitzpatrick Referrals in Surrey, where orthopaedic-neuro veterinary surgeon Noel Fitzpatrick and his team help some of the nation's hardest-to-cure pets using cutting-edge procedures. A woman arrives with 14 of her rescue dogs, including a tiny papillon in need of a double knee replacement. Noel and his team also fit a German shepherd with a specially designed bionic foot, and treat a mountain dog that is suffering problems with its spine

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: When Erin and JB can't fix the doors back onto their solarium, Dick Strawbridge steps in to help them find a solution to their rusted hinges. Another leak is found, but this time from the roof, so they call in some professional climbers to scale their bell tower to find the source of the problem. With wedding season over, Tim and Margreeth launch a foodie weekend. With a professional chef hired to produce the food and some trainee butlers on hand to serve more than 20 guests, the couple can take it easy - apart from foraging for mushrooms, dressing the dining room and fixing a guest's belt. Meanwhile, May and her son Jack get the yoga room ready for the imminent arrival of guests.

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Stuart and Rosie Treasurer from the Wirral plan to transform a small bungalow from a 1960s dormer into a slick piece of 21st-century architecture. The couple decapitate the building and balance a new floating timber box on top to create five bedrooms, spending as little as possible on plumbing, electrics and insulation, but stress levels spiral when neighbours grumble at the ultra-modern creation

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: The architect meets a teacher planning to turn a double-decker bus into a mobile classroom, while a family of car enthusiasts from Coventry is working on a camping trailer. Plus, the Spanish tour continues with a visit to a 100-year-old farmhouse on the coast of Asturias, and the Big Build team design a lightweight, flexible and multi-functional wall system for the campervan living pod

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Young parents Nicole and Stefian arrive in resus with their 19-month-old daughter Xah'Nae, who is unresponsive and has a high fever, and doctors fear she may have a life-threatening infection. Meanwhile, the team suspects 61-year-old Christine has suffered a second stroke in 10 years, and 52-year-old Graham, who has Down's syndrome, is brought into A&E by his sisters following an accident

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy panel show's version of the words-and-numbers quiz, as Jon Richardson and David Mitchell take on guest captain Joe Wilkinson and Roisin Conaty. Tom Allen joins Countdown's resident lexicographer Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner and Rachel Riley looks after the numbers and letters

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: Key striker Father Jack drinks himself into oblivion just as the Craggy Island clerics are about to play a vital five-a-side football match against their Rugged Island rivals, led by devious Dick Byrne. Dermot Morgan stars

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: Cheating in the football match backfires on Ted, prompting his Rugged Island rivals to think up a devilish forfeit - and leaving the priest trying to contrive a way of kicking Bishop Brennan's backside and getting away with it. Comedy, starring Dermot Morgan and Ardal O'Hanlon

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: Heather and Nigel Holmes fancy a brand-new life in a modern property with amazing views in the Mijas area of Spain. However, as Heather's mum is in a home in Hampshire, they would also like a smaller base near to her for when they visit the UK. Jasmine Harman and Jonnie Irwin help them find the best of both

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien arrive in York, where Henry struggles to squeeze into some motorcycle leathers and Simon gets into deep water when he tries to restore an aluminium boat

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien visit a stately home in Somerset, where Simon is convinced he has uncovered a genuine Tudor relic

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: When Erin and JB can't fix the doors back onto their solarium, Dick Strawbridge steps in to help them find a solution to their rusted hinges. With wedding season over, Tim and Margreeth launch a foodie weekend. With a professional chef hired to produce the food and some trainee butlers on hand to serve more than 20 guests, the couple can take it easy - apart from foraging for mushrooms, dressing the dining room and fixing a guest's belt. Meanwhile, May and her son Jack get the yoga room ready for the imminent arrival of guests.

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: Charlie Luxton helps Andrew and Vicki Dodden from Cannington, Somerset, who plan to transform an 18th-century cottage into an eco-friendly home that is suitable for modern living. The couple stand to save £150,000 by opting to self-build, and visit a nearby house in Wells for inspiration on how they can tweak the design of their conversion to get the very best results

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition heads to Spain, where Ben Eyre and Anna Chalu are first to host at Vista Del Torcal in the Andalusian Mountains. Despite being impressed with their rooms, some guests are less enthused by an afternoon of rock climbing, leaving Ben and Anna hoping a full English breakfast will save their bacon

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Jed and Mandy Filmer welcome their rivals to the Hotel la Tartana on Spain's Costa Tropical, and hope to impress them with a cookery class - not realising one of their guests is a qualified chef. A revelation at dinner stuns the group, before the Filmers' attitude to breakfast service jeopardises their chances of victory

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Julia Grant hosts the third round of the competition in Spain at her Benidorm B&B The Queens Hotel. Her guests are not impressed with her standards of cleanliness, finding dirt, dust and hairs, and tensions rise at dinner over her 'straight-friendly' label. The owner improves the evening by giving everyone a drag makeover and persuading them to perform in a revue, but some of the feedback she receives leaves her enraged

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The week's final hosts in Spain are Alastair Smith and Martyn Watson, owners of the gay and naturist-friendly Almond Tree Villa in Murcia. The day's activity is olive picking, but the mood turns sour when Jed admits he gave Julia's hotel low marks, while a disaster in the bathroom and a sugar-rich continental breakfast reflect badly on Alastair and Martyn's feedback scores

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants of the Spanish special meet one last time to learn how much they have been paid by their fellow hoteliers before discovering who has won the week's competition. Old tensions resurface as the results are announced, and there are heated exchanges around the table

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Pregnant giraffe Orla goes into labour, but the keepers must watch from the sidelines due to the risks involved during the birth. Baby Bornean orang-utan Tombol tries his luck with the gibbons as he heads out and explores his new habitat. Elsewhere, northern bald ibis chick Professor X is proving too confident for his own good, and when it is time to release him into the adult habitat he gets into troubled waters and suffers a nasty head injury, so the vets have to work hard to help him pull through

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Expats Dave and Sue arrive at the surgery after a 500-mile trip from France to bring their border collie Kaspar, who has a leg pain that is proving hard to diagnose. An eight-year-old pug needs surgery on its elbow, and an Egyptian Mau cat is rushed into Fitzpatrick's after fracturing its knee in a fall from a second-floor balcony. Noel goes ahead with surgery on the complex fracture, but if there are any complications the kitten may face amputation

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Erin and JB launch their chateau business by opening it up for a tea party for the locals, including the mayor. May holds her health and wellbeing retreat using her new yoga studio for the first time

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud follows a project to renovate and extend a classic 1920s cinema in Thorne, South Yorkshire. With little knowledge of restoration, Gwyn and Kate boldly set about turning the dilapidated building into a family home using masses of concrete after taking advice from an architect friend. The couple are hoping to create a hydraulic glass wall that opens up one side of the house to form a UV-filtered canopy, and there are also plans for a white roof extension that resembles a diving board

programme.title: Code 37: Sex Crimes
programme.desc: Part one of two. The team receives a video delivered anonymously and depicting a pregnant woman being raped. Charles and Bob recognise the woman as prostitute Lily Laets. Her lover Max says that she agreed to collaborate and Lily confirms his story, but Hannah doubts the truth of Lily's statement. Belgian crime thriller, starring Veerle Baetens, Michael Pas, Marc Lauwrys and Gilles De Schryver. In Flemish

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Bill, 92, arrives at King's with breathing difficulties and a swollen leg and test results suggest there may be something seriously wrong, while 25-year-old Sarah is finding it hard to stand up and has been brought in by her mum and grandfather. She has learning difficulties as a result of an inherited genetic disorder and doctors are concerned she may have suffered a stroke

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Peggy, 85, arrives at the hospital by ambulance, having injured her knee at home. It's the second time she has fallen in two months and medics want to find out if there's a more serious underlying cause. Meanwhile, the team keeps a close eye on 75-year-old Bob, who has had a suspected heart attack, and Ben, 25, isn't overly concerned by his swollen finger, until a doctor diagnoses cellulitis

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to Baltimore, Maryland, where Denise Whiting has alienated customers by trademarking the word 'hon', a term of endearment used by the local community. When she opened Cafe Hon in 1992 it was a roaring success, but as a result of Denise's unwavering business ambition, people started to boycott the restaurant, leaving her frustrated by falling sales

programme.desc: The guys find a replica spaceship during a picking trip in Louisiana, and Mike employs some new negotiating tactics to get a suitable price

programme.title: Storage Hunters
programme.desc: Jesse forges controversial alliances, while Sean uncovers an auction lot that has been used for a prank

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: Mike and Frank find a classic 33 Ford that has been sitting so long that a tree has grown around it, but nothing stops the guys from rescuing this rare ride

programme.title: American Pickers

programme.desc: Officers are on the lookout for day-trippers trying to smuggle prescription pills back into America, bought cheaply from one of the 200 pharmacies on the other side of the border

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: Claustrophobic prison cells that contain a story worthy of international acclaim, and a vast mountainside complex that held the power to change the world

programme.title: Travel Man: 48 Hours In
programme.desc: Richard Ayoade and Robert Webb explore the Portuguese island, including sampling the scary-looking black scabbard fish with banana at a remote seafront restaurant that can be reached only by sea or cable car. They also head to Funchal for an embroidery lesson and muse over the merits of a Madeiran mini-break while enjoying afternoon tea on the terrace

programme.title: Travel Man: 48 Hours In
programme.desc: Richard Ayoade is joined by comedian Shazia Mirza for a two-day break in the French Riviera, hiring a three-wheeled car for a scenic tour of Nice as well as checking out Cap Ferrat, blue art, fancy yachts and a grotto in Monaco. Later, they take a pricey helicopter ride before they end with a trip to the casino - hoping to win back the budget overspend on the helicopter

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef embarks on another round of extended culinary breaks, visiting markets, restaurants, wineries, cafes and bars, beginning with Portuguese capital Lisbon. The culinary effects of Portugal's historic explorations are still seen in its dishes with hints of cinnamon and freshly chopped coriander from the east and tomatoes and chillies from the Americas. Rick is inspired to cook salt cod fritters and an almond tart, before heading for the St Anthony's Day Parade and Sardine Festival

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson subjects the Skoda Yeti to a thorough examination, while Richard Hammond charts the evolution of the Porsche 911 by pitting a modern Turbo S cabriolet against its oldest relative the Volkswagen Beetle - and the power of gravity. James May takes the Ariel Atom V8 for a spin on the test track, and comedian John Bishop receives tuition from the Stig before getting behind the wheel of the Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The presenters square up to their Australian counterparts in an automotive version of the Ashes, with challenges including rallying, drifting and a double-decker car race. Jeremy Clarkson also test-drives the Ferrari 599 GTO, and Boris Becker gets behind the wheel of the Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: A compilation of extra material from the comedy panel show, hosted by Rob Brydon. Team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack are joined by guests including Claudia Winkleman, Miles Jupp, Bob Mortimer, Jo Brand, Adil Ray, Kirsty Wark, Amanda Abbington and Carol Vorderman to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves

They join the other 26.7 million tourists, as they gorge themselves on questionable foodstuffs, traditional transportation and the world's largest seated Buddha statue. After resting at the Peninsula Hotel for some colonial opulence, they sample street food and get their fortunes read, before taking to the air for a bird's eye view by helicopter

programme.title: QI XL

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef visits the home of New Nordic cuisine - Copenhagen - where he goes foraging for rosehip and learns about the art of pickling buds, bark and flowers. He's cheered to find plenty of traditional favourites, such as the city's famous Smorrebrod open sandwiches on rye bread, delicious poached turbot with cabbage and lemon butter sauce and - of course - the ubiquitous Danish pastry. Back at home, Rick cooks fried pork belly with lovage potatoes and parsley sauce, which has been voted Denmark's favourite dish

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May visit Albania to test three luxury vehicles - the Rolls-Royce Ghost, Bentley Mulsanne and Mercedes-Benz S65 AMG. Jeremy also drives three high-performance hatchbacks in the form of the limited-edition Ford Focus RS500, Prodrive-developed Cosworth Impreza STi CS400 and one-off Volvo C30 PPC. Jonathan Ross is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Top Gear

Their two-day break in Zurich features trips to a football museum and the Cabaret Voltaire, which was the birthplace of the Dada art movement, while they also go on the steepest funicular railway in the world, pay homage to the Swiss army knife and go for a pedalo ride on Lake Zurich

programme.title: Travel Man: 48 Hours In
programme.desc: Richard Ayoade is joined by writer and actor Jessica Knappett for his latest trip, touring the island in a silver Citroen 2CV, discovering there is more to Ibiza than foam parties, and visiting the Unesco world heritage site of Dalt Vila in the capital. They also take in al fresco art at the island's version of Stonehenge, experience an exclusive supper club run by the global superstar chef Boris Buono and sample a selection of pricy cocktails, flowriding and a hippy gong bath

programme.title: American Pickers

programme.title: Travel Man: 48 Hours In
programme.desc: Crystal Maze presenter Richard Ayoade returns with the offbeat tourism show and is joined in the first edition by comedian and Room 101 host Frank Skinner for a short break in Switzerland's largest city. Their two-day break in Zurich features trips to a football museum and the Cabaret Voltaire, which was the birthplace of the Dada art movement, while they also go on the steepest funicular railway in the world, pay homage to the Swiss army knife and go for a pedalo ride on Lake Zurich

programme.title: Travel Man: 48 Hours In
programme.desc: Richard Ayoade is joined by writer and actor Jessica Knappett for his latest trip, touring the island in a silver Citroen 2CV, discovering there is more to Ibiza than foam parties, and visiting the Unesco world heritage site of Dalt Vila in the capital. They also take in al fresco art at the island's version of Stonehenge, experience an exclusive supper club run by the global superstar chef Boris Buono and sample a selection of pricy cocktails, flowriding and a hippy gong bath

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef heads to Cadiz in Spain, where he samples two of the city's most revered culinary stars - fresh tuna and Manzanilla sherry. Rick is captivated by the narrow winding streets as he walks in the footsteps of the ancient Phoenician and Arab traders, whose influence on the city can still be seen to this day. Back home, he prepares flamenco eggs and arroz verde, or green rice, made with garlic, parsley, clams and prawns, producing the vibrant colours of the Spanish flag

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Presenters Jeremy Clarkson, James May and Richard Hammond tackle the traffic chaos snowy weather causes in Britain, and hit upon a solution involving a combine harvester, some rudimentary engineering skills and a trip to Norway. Jeremy also tests the Audi RS5 and the BMW M3, and American actress and model Amber Heard is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson gets behind the wheel of a Jaguar XJ and races against the rotation of the Earth. James May travels to America to drive a Nasa Space Exploration Vehicle and Richard Hammond tests two of the most famous supercars of the 1980s - the Porsche 959 and the Ferrari F40. John Prescott is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Red Dwarf
programme.desc: Rimmer finally tires of being embarrassed by his fellow crewmen, and decides to take his revenge by forcing them to spend 12 weeks in strict quarantine after they are exposed to a deadly virus. Sci-fi comedy, starring Craig Charles and Chris Barrie

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson sets off on a 45-mile hike through the countryside of the Weald in Kent and the Downs of East Sussex to discover the area's rich and surprising Tudor heritage. At Penshurst Place, author Philippa Gregory helps him relish the fate of the Grand Duke of Buckingham at the hands of the young Henry VIII. From there, he travels up what used to be secret paths to Hever Castle and finds out how the monarch's reign brought not only huge social, political and industrial change, but also fame and disaster to the women who caught his eye He ends this leg of his journey in Lewes, where he relives one of the more brutal monastic dissolutions

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson goes on a four-day trek through the Kintail region of the north-western Highlands, examining the story of the Jacobite uprisings of the early 1700s along the way. He starts by visiting Iron Age dwellings in Shiel Bridge, and heads to the rebuilt castle on Eilean Donan, the original of which was destroyed after invading Spanish troops landed there and were attacked by British warships. Tony ends in Glen Shiel, the site of a climactic battle in 1719 where royalist troops defeated the rebels

programme.title: Forbidden History
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston investigates what Hitler intended to do with the priceless works of art stolen by the Nazis from across Europe, as well as his obsession with religious artefacts

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: Laurence Olivier narrates the story of the D-Day landings in June 1944, when the Allies launched the biggest ever amphibious invasion on the beaches of Normandy. Although both sides suffered heavy casualties, it proved to be the turning point in the war against Germany

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the second of two programmes from Bowood House, Wiltshire, and hears the story of a man who went in search of his biological father after finding letters to his mother from an American soldier. The experts examine a box engraved with a secret code, a cigarette case made by a Russian craftsman, a collection of transistor radios and a cartoon based on a news story about an escaped lion that hid in a school

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: Hidden tunnels that saw a city prosper, a sunken wreck with a remarkable past, a concrete wartime dome and a mysterious Cold War building

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo explores the Victorians' fascination with antiquity as he continues his tour of the Republic of Ireland and Northern Ireland. He visits the cromlech stones of Dundalk, Co Louth, and an observatory in Co Armagh, and travels on the steam railway in Downpatrick, Co Down

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo explores the rich history of Belfast's Victorian docks, learns about a 150-year-old spade-making tradition and takes a walk along coastal paths of Whitehead, Co Antrim

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents an episode focused on Indian art and culture from the Neasden Temple in North London, revealing the connections between British and Indian history. Among the artefacts being valued are ceremonial robes used in the Delhi Durbar, a ring given to a boy by a princess, a Rolls Royce made for the Maharajah of Jodhpur and a bracelet found in a children's toy box that turns out to have a surprising background

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Four VW camper vans pitch up at the auction house looking for a new home and Derek picks up an Austin 16 that 'goes like stink and has some unusual modern modifications'

programme.title: Impossible Railways
programme.desc: Since their rise in the 19th century, railways have been at the forefront of modern engineering and transport, conquering dizzying altitudes, wilderness, seas and cities

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: A village ruin that is now pitted and scarred, a ghostly fleet of ships, a bridge to nowhere hidden in a forest and strange metal structures in a wood

programme.title: World War Weird
programme.desc: The story of an RAF pilot with incredible night vision, which supposedly came from eating carrots. Plus, rebels in the Philippines who believed they were fighting a vampire

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: The Nazi occupation of Holland, which began relatively peacefully with Dutch civil servants continuing to run their own country, but eventually resulted in many thousands of deaths through the attempted elimination of Jewish citizens and starvation of the non-Jewish inhabitants. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo ends his journey across the Republic of Ireland and Northern Ireland by walking across the Carrick-a-Rede rope bridge in Co Antrim. He then follows in the footsteps of the Victorians at Giant's Causeway, and explores the history of Londonderry

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo sets off from Ayr as he embarks on the first leg of a journey through southern Scotland from west to east, admiring the granite island of Ailsa Craig in the Firth of Clyde before getting to grips with the sport of curling, with a little help from a Scottish world champion. The Ancient Society of Kilwinning Archers invites Michael to take part in a competition, at Barassie he rides the footplate of a freight train hauling coal on Scotland's oldest railway line as he heads on towards Stewarton

programme.title: War Factories
programme.desc: The incredible story of the arms race to create America's nuclear arms factories

programme.title: Dad's Army: The Lost Episodes
programme.desc: When he's told he can make a member of the platoon a corporal, Mainwaring instigates a competition between Frazer and Jones to see who deserves the promotion. Both do their best to impress him, particularly during a demonstration on how to face down a Nazi stormtrooper, but things go awry when a power-crazed Frazer charges the other men with court-martial offences. Remake of the classic sitcom, starring Kevin McNally, Robert Bathurst, Kevin Eldon and David Hayman

programme.title: Open All Hours
programme.desc: Arkwright decides Granville needs toughening up and starts dragging him around the store by his ear, but the regular customers are unhappy by what they see

programme.title: Open All Hours

programme.title: Open All Hours

The chameleon has an extra-long tongue that helps it catch prey and the giraffe's neck is so long it is easily able to reach the tops of trees for food

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson embarks on a 45-mile walk across Wiltshire, from Avebury to Stonehenge, telling the story of the remarkable development that occurred in the latter days of the Neolithic era. His route over chalk downlands and Salisbury Plain takes him through one of the greatest concentrations of prehistoric sites in Europe

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: It was 30 years after the Romans invaded Britain that they were ready to take on the challenge of conquering the Lake District. With the toughest landscape they had encountered in the nation, peopled by a rebellious tribe, it was no small task, but within a few years, the whole of Lakeland was under their control. Tony Robinson tackles the journey by himself, walking 50 miles from Penrith to Ambleside and on to the Irish Sea at Ravenglass, discovering that the Romans encountered beauty and danger in equal measure

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: The team struggles to explain how a DAF 33 gearbox works, and office manager Sarah attempts to bring order to the auto memorabilia sale

programme.title: Impossible Railways
programme.desc: How some of our greatest railways have challenged engineering's finest minds in their efforts to conquer the planet's waterways, including a tunnel beneath the English Channel.

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: An eerie, empty country village which hides a sad secret, a mysterious tower battered by the elements and a disused railway deep beneath the streets of a city

programme.title: World War Weird
programme.desc: How the Japanese air force dropped swarms of fleas on a Chinese city in a bid to infect the population with bubonic plague

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: Laurence Olivier narrates an account of the liberation of Paris and Brussels in 1944, events which to many signified the end of the war. However, as the German forces retreated, Poland fell victim to one of the most tragic events of the war, Documentary chronicling the Second World War

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his journey through southern Scotland, with this leg beginning in the industrial town of Greenock, from where he sets sail in the world's last sea-going paddle steamer. He then sees the devastating effects of a fire at the Glasgow School of Art, heads to Blantyre to find out about the humble beginnings of a famous missionary and explorer and learns to make sourdough in a bakery in Strathaven before finishing his trip in Larkhall

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo sets off from Motherwell as he continues his journey through southern Scotland, celebrating the Victorian iron and steel industry and admires one of its crowning achievements - the Forth Rail Bridge. He then visits the Falls of Clyde, which inspired Wordsworth and Coleridge, heads to Cumbernauld to learn about the origins of one of Scotland's best-selling soft drinks, and in Linlithgow he marvels at the ingenuity of the engineers who built the Union Canal

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: An abandoned railway station near a town in Spain that is home to just 500 people and a record-breaking Arctic facility that goes deep underground

programme.title: Dad's Army: The Lost Episodes
programme.desc: Tipped off by Frazer and Godfrey, Mainwaring and Wilson investigate a house from where they believe someone may have been signalling to enemy aircraft. They take the suspect back to the church hall, but chaos ensues when an incendiary bomb comes through the roof and the platoon desperately battles the blaze. Remake of the classic sitcom, starring Kevin McNally, Robert Bathurst and Kevin Eldon

programme.title: Train Truckers
programme.desc: Zak and Alex face a four-day mission to deliver a pair of classic steam locomotives for an Autumn Steam Gala at the Mid Hants Railway in Hampshire

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: The drop in morale of Churchill's forces following the fall of France to Nazi forces during the Second World War, and how the Dunkirk evacuation and the Battle of Britain helped to restore British confidence. Plus, America's reasons for initially refusing to enter the conflict

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson takes a four-day trek along the coastline between Plymouth in Devon and Falmouth, learning about the thriving smuggling trade in Cornwall during the late 18th century. The area's tiny secretive harbours, beaches and secluded coves were ideal for illicit imports such as tobacco and brandy, and the business was so huge it threatened the national economy. Along his route, visiting such beauty spots as Lantic Bay and Polperro, he encounters all sorts of reminders of the trade and meets descendants of those involved

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson embarks on another expedition through some of Britain's most historic landscapes. He begins with a towpath exploration of industrial engineering along the Leeds and Liverpool Canal. Setting off from Liverpool, he takes a three-day walk inland to Wigan, ending at the town's iconic pier as he traces the story of the waterway's chaotic construction and its impact on the development of Lancashire

programme.title: Forbidden History
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston investigates the Dead Sea Scrolls, visiting the caves where they were discovered to find out who wrote them and why they were hidden

John Axford challenges Fiona to guess the odd one out from four tea bowls, and Jon Baddeley values a model ship that took more than 30 years to build

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Anglophile Derek struggles to get his message across on a rare trip abroad to pick up a Bentley, while a well-restored Ford XR2 sets the auction alight

programme.title: Impossible Railways
programme.desc: A look at the planet's most incredible metropolitan railways, and how they help people solve distinctly urban problems. Including a project to upgrade New York's transit system.

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: A settlement in Argentina destroyed by floods, a town in the US that has been erased, and a Soviet dream-town that turned into a nuclear nightmare

programme.title: Hitler's England
programme.desc: Former Bergerac star John Nettles returns to Jersey to tell the story of the British Channel Islands under German occupation from 1940 to 1945

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: The story of the Nazi quest to establish an Aryan race, which set out to subjugate the Jewish population with decrees and laws about citizenship and escalated to the use of execution camps and the horrors of the Holocaust. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo begins this leg of his journey in Stirling, where he visits the scene of the Battle of Bannockburn before travelling north to Crieff in Perth and Kinross to take in a Victorian spa. In Perth, he finds out how to make a sporran and catches the Highland Main Line to Pitlochry and one of Queen Victoria's favourite haunts, then finishes with a dram in Scotland's smallest distillery

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on the final leg of his journey across southern Scotland from west to east, beginning by paying homage to the birthplace of golf at St Andrews. In Dunfermline, he learns about the humble origins of a remarkable philanthropist who worked on the railroads before making his fortune in steel, and then crosses the Forth Bridge to end his trip in Edinburgh, where he treads the boards in an unconventional adaptation of a play by Oscar Wilde at the Festival Fringe

programme.title: Wonders of the Universe
programme.desc: Professor Brian Cox explores how gravity influences events across the universe, discovering why such a relatively weak force dictates Earth's orbit around the Sun - as well as the solar system's movement through the galaxy. He also explains why the gravity of a neutron star is much stronger than that of Earth, and looks back through history to reveal why scientists' research into the power of attraction has given mankind a far greater understanding of the cosmos

programme.title: Restoration Workshop
programme.desc: Gary Wallis re-invents an old ships compass and a grand display cabinet into two of his most successful transformations to date

programme.title: War Factories

programme.desc: Fiery chef Gordon Ramsay comes to the aid of the owners of Hannah & Mason's, a French bistro in Cranbury, New Jersey. Members of staff complain their pay cheques bounce, and the kitchen cooler is so unhygienic that Gordon makes the decision to close the restaurant on Valentine's Day - the busiest night of the year

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to Atlanta, Georgia, in an attempt to save a family run smokehouse restaurant plagued by mismanagement and lazy cooking. Michon's owner, Al Wilson, had always hoped he could pass his beloved business on to daughter Natalie, but when he suffered a collapsed lung the handover came about much earlier than expected. Now it's down to Gordon to motivate Natalie and save Michon's from disaster

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
